title: Support reverse cumulative scans
description: JAX lax.cumsum/lax.cumprod accept reverse=false by default and reverse=true for suffix cumulative scans. Add reverse param parsing in fj-lax, trace validation, and conformance coverage.
close_reason: Completed reverse=true support for lax.cumsum/lax.cumprod with eval, trace validation, and conformance coverage.

title: fj-lax reduce_window silently ignored base_dilation/window_dilation it does not implement
description: eval_reduce_window (crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs) and the fj-trace ReduceWindow shape inference read window_dimensions+window_strides+padding+reduce_op but SILENTLY IGNORED base_dilation (dilates the input) and window_dilation (dilates the window). Dilated/atrous pooling would return a wrong (undilated) result for the same shape -- a silent parity violation vs jax.lax.reduce_window, no error. Same footgun class as conv (jc5b1). These keys appear nowhere else in the codebase so no current caller is affected. FIX: reject non-default base_dilation/window_dilation loudly in eval (EvalError::Unsupported) + trace (TraceError::ShapeInferenceFailed); default all-1/absent still succeeds. Test reduce_window_rejects_unimplemented_dilation. Part of the 'reads subset of params, silently ignores rest' hardening sweep; pad correctly handles padding_interior (not a footgun); gather/scatter still to audit.

title: [testing-metamorphic] pad recovery relations lack conformance coverage
description: P1 finding from empty-backlog /testing-metamorphic rotation. pad_oracle covers fixed examples for low/high/interior padding, but it does not assert the cross-primitive recovery relations that make pad semantics robust: (1) edge pad followed by slicing away the added low/high region recovers the operand, and (2) interior padding followed by strided slicing at original element positions recovers the operand. Repro: inspect crates/fj-conformance/tests/pad_oracle.rs; there are oracle examples for pad and separate slice metamorphic tests, but no test fails if Pad places original elements at the wrong offset while preserving the hand-written examples. Fix in same tick: add pad/slice metamorphic recovery tests and validate with rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test pad_oracle.
close_reason: Added pad/slice metamorphic recovery coverage for edge and interior padding; validated with rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test pad_oracle and clippy.

title: [bug] fj-dispatch Eigh batching tests assert arbitrary eigenvector sign
description: RCH full lib gate for z92vn exposed two unrelated failures. Command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-z92vn-test-20260605 rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-dispatch --lib on worker ts2. Failing tests: batching::tests::test_batch_trace_eigh_multi_leading_batch_dim and batching::tests::test_batch_trace_eigh_multi_nonleading_batch_dim. Both fail at crates/fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs:7325 comparing -0.7071067811865475 to 0.7071067811865475. Eigenvector signs are mathematically arbitrary, so the tests should compare sign-invariant eigenspaces/reconstruction or the implementation should enforce a deterministic orientation if that is the intended public contract. This is unrelated to z92vn I64 axis-1 Add but blocks cargo test -p fj-dispatch --lib.
notes: FIXED ea7e46fd (test-only): assert_eigh_matches_slice_oracle now compares eigenvalues element-wise and eigenvector COLUMNS up to ± sign. Root cause: batch_eigh_multi (fj-dispatch Jacobi) and eval_primitive_multi(Eigh) (fj-lax eigh) are independent valid eigensolvers → arbitrary per-column eigenvector sign (-0.7071 vs +0.7071). Spectrum/ordering pinned by eigenvalue compare; batched bits stay pinned by golden_sha256 tests (untouched). fj-dispatch --lib gate restored: 260 passed, 0 failed.

title: [code-review] optimize_jaxpr default remains aggressive after safe-mode hardening
description: Follow-up from the last-six-hours egraph commits: public dispatch now calls optimize_jaxpr_with_config(..., OptimizationConfig::safe()) when egraph_optimize=true, but the public fj_egraph::optimize_jaxpr() and algebraic_rules() helpers still use OptimizationConfig::default(), and Default currently means numerical_safety_mode=false. That keeps bare optimize_jaxpr on the aggressive rule set after the safe-mode hardening work. Repro: build log(exp(x)) or sin(x)^2+cos(x)^2 as a Jaxpr, call fj_egraph::optimize_jaxpr directly, and evaluate with x=1000.0 or +Inf; the default optimizer may return 1000.0 or 1.0 instead of preserving +Inf/NaN. Expected: the default semantics-preserving helper is safe by default, or the aggressive API is explicitly named/documented and all tests that depend on unsafe rewrites opt into OptimizationConfig::aggressive(). Update conformance helper coverage so semantics-preservation tests do not accidentally exercise aggressive mode under a safe-sounding name.
close_reason: Fixed review findings: egraph lowering now returns typed errors for malformed variables/arity, optimize_jaxpr defaults to safe mode, and conformance coverage opts unsafe rewrites into aggressive mode.

title: Pin Round signed-zero oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX jnp.round is the NumPy round implementation and lowers decimal-zero float rounding through lax.round(..., TO_NEAREST_EVEN); lax.round also exposes AWAY_FROM_ZERO and TO_NEAREST_EVEN stablehlo round methods. Upstream docs show TO_NEAREST_EVEN producing -0. for -0.5, and NumPy preserves IEEE signed zero for round(+0.0) and round(-0.0). FrankenJAX conformance currently checks scalar round(+/-0.0) by numeric equality only. Add exact signed-zero bit-pattern oracle coverage in fj-conformance.
close_reason: Pinned Round signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with RCH: cargo test -p fj-conformance --test round_oracle.

title: [code-review] safe egraph select rewrites skip predicate validation
description:
Review evidence from the last-six-hours safe-egraph hardening:
- safe_algebraic_rules still includes select-true, select-false, and select-same in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.
- fj-lax eval_select validates the condition and rejects complex conditions with 'select condition must be boolean, got complex dtype' in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs. It also validates scalar/tensor kind and shape compatibility across the condition and branch values.
- select-same rewrites select(c, a, a) directly to a. With a complex condition, the original program fails closed, while the safe/default optimized program can return a without evaluating the invalid predicate.
- select-true/select-false have the same validation-skipping shape/kind risk for malformed branch inputs, because the original select checks matching scalar/tensor kinds and branch dtype promotion even when the selected predicate is a literal.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph regression that builds out = Select(cond, x, x) with invars [cond, x].
2. Evaluate the original with args [Value::scalar_complex128(1.0, 0.0), Value::scalar_f64(7.0)]. Expected: eval_jaxpr returns the existing fj-lax TypeMismatch for complex select condition.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe()) and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with the same args. Current safe/default can apply select-same and return Value::scalar_f64(7.0), converting a fail-closed input error into success.
4. Add a literal-condition malformed-branch case such as Select(Literal::I64(1), scalar, tensor) to cover select-true/false skipping matching-kind validation.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_select_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph select_validation -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: remove select-same/select-true/select-false from safe mode unless the egraph carries enough type/shape/predicate validity evidence, or keep them only in a typed lowering phase after validation has already been proven.
close_reason: Moved validation-skipping select rewrites out of safe/default egraph rules and added select validation regressions

title: FileCache corrupt reads should evict malformed wire entries
description: Review-mode finding: FileCache::get returns a corrupt sentinel for malformed serialized cache files, and CacheManager::get surfaces CacheLookup::Corrupted, but the corrupt .bin remains on disk. The P2C005 contract and CacheLookup docs say corrupt entries are evicted, so repeated lookups keep rediscovering the same bad entry instead of becoming a miss. Fix by evicting malformed file-backed cache entries after surfacing corruption once, and add focused regressions.

title: [reality-check] support jax.pmap for multi-device parallelism
description:
JAX's pmap (parallel map) enables SPMD execution across multiple devices.

**Gap:** Not implemented. Transform enum only has Jit/Grad/Vmap.

**Effort:** Large - requires multi-device backend, collective operations (psum, pmean, etc.).

**Prerequisites:** GPU/TPU backend (frankenjax-82az).

**JAX reference:** `jax.pmap`, `jax.lax.psum`, `jax.lax.pmean`

title: fj-ad: clamp grad (VJP+JVP) errors for tensor operand / scalar bounds (jnp.clip)
description: Clamp VJP+JVP built masks via value_bool_and = select(lhs, rhs, scalar_false). When operand is a tensor, the AND of two tensor masks is select(Tensor,Tensor,Scalar) which eval_select rejects (lax.select is strict same-shape, no T/T/S arm). So grad of clamp/jnp.clip with a tensor operand (the normal case, incl. scalar bounds clip(x,0,1)) errored. Existing tests only used scalar inputs so it hid. Fixed: clamp_active_indicators builds 0/1 indicators via broadcasting multiply (select(mask,1,0) then mul) instead of select/AND; grad = g*indicator, reverse pass sums bound cotangents to scalar. Removed value_bool_and/value_masked.
close_reason: Fixed: clamp_active_indicators builds 0/1 indicators via broadcasting multiply (no strict-shape select/AND of bool masks). clamp VJP+JVP no longer error for tensor operand / scalar bounds. Test test_grad_clamp_scalar_bounds_does_not_error (VJP+JVP) passes. fj-ad 334/0, conformance ad 93/0.

title: Refactor fj-conformance ad_numerical_verification RFFT F32 dtype test to use validate_dtype_consistency
description: ad_numerical_verification.rs:1005-1010 iterates RFFT VJP cotangent elements with matches!(elem, Literal::F32Bits(_)) — replace with vjp_tensor.validate_dtype_consistency() to consolidate the dtype-element invariant check, following the go38/n43l/u46o/etnz pattern.
notes: Replaced the RFFT F32 dtype/element matches! loop in ad_numerical_verification.rs with vjp_tensor.validate_dtype_consistency() — matches the go38/n43l/u46o/etnz refactor pattern. ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: fj-ad: rem grad wrt divisor used floor instead of trunc (wrong for negative quotient)
description: Rem VJP and JVP computed d/db (a%b) = -floor(a/b)*g. But lax.rem (and fj-lax rem, Rust %) is C-style truncated: rem(a,b)=a-trunc(a/b)*b, so the grad is -trunc(a/b). For a/b<0 floor!=trunc: rem(-7,3) d/db gave +3 instead of JAX's +2. Fixed both rules Floor->Trunc (matches JAX -sign(a/b)*floor(|a/b|)=-trunc).
close_reason: Fixed: Rem VJP+JVP use Trunc(a/b) not Floor(a/b), matching JAX -sign(a/b)*floor(|a/b|)=-trunc and fj-lax's truncated rem. TDD repro test_rem_grad_db_uses_truncated_quotient (rem(-7,3) db=+2) failed (got 3) before, passes after. fj-ad 330/0.

title: Fix fj-lax eval_clamp mixed-dtype tensor element widening to F64
description: eval_clamp at crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:1605 has a 'mixed types' fallback that returns Literal::from_f64. Tensor callers (lines 1682-1687, 1708-1712) use x.dtype / lo.dtype for the TensorValue, so a tensor of declared dtype F32/BF16/F16 ends up with F64 element literals whenever the scalar lo/hi bounds are a different dtype (common because constants default to F64). Same invariant violation as 2chb/eldm. Convert mixed-arm output via real_literal_from_f64(target_dtype, ...) using the tensor's dtype.
notes: clamp_literal now accepts an Option<DType> target dtype; tensor callers pass Some(tensor.dtype). The mixed-types arm routes through real_literal_from_f64 so BF16/F16/F32 tensors no longer end up with F64Bits elements when scalar bounds are F64. fj-lax 761 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: Support boolean which in SelectN parity

Current fj-lax eval_select_n requires idx_lit.as_i64() and rejects Literal::Bool with 'select_n index must be an integer'. Align Primitive::SelectN with upstream boolean-which semantics, reject boolean which with >2 cases, and add fj-lax/fj-conformance regressions.

title: fj-ad: gather/scatter JVP used the index tangent (zeros) instead of primal indices
description: Gather JVP passed tangents[1..] (the index tangents, which are zero for integer indices) as the gather indices, so it gathered at index 0 for every output position. Scatter JVP likewise scattered at index 0. Correct: d gather(op,idx)=gather(d_op, idx) and d scatter(op,idx,upd)=scatter(d_op, idx, d_upd) — at the PRIMAL indices. So forward-mode AD through gather/scatter (indexing) was fundamentally broken. gather JVP not in jvp_numerical_verification, so it hid. Fixed both to use primals[1] for indices.
close_reason: Fixed: gather/scatter JVP use primals[1] (primal indices), not the zero index tangent. Test test_gather_scatter_jvp_use_primal_indices_not_index_tangent (gather [3,1], scatter [0.1,0.2,5.0]) passes. fj-ad 344/0, conformance jvp 76/0.

title: Implement U32/U64 unary arithmetic in eval_unary_int_or_float (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Handle Literal::U32 and Literal::U64 in eval_unary_int_or_float() at crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:942-945.
WHERE: The match arm at line 942 returns "unsigned unary arithmetic not yet implemented" for both U32 and U64.
HOW: For each unary op that calls this function:
  - neg: U32/U64 negation is wrapping in JAX (twos complement). Implement as wrapping_neg() or cast to i64, negate, cast back. Check JAX oracle behavior.
  - abs: Identity for unsigned types (always non-negative). Return input unchanged.
  - sign: 0 for 0, 1 for any positive value. Trivial for unsigned.
  - Other unary ops routed here should be checked case-by-case.
Check which callers use eval_unary_int_or_float: grep for it and ensure each caller passes appropriate uint_op alongside int_op and float_op. May need to add a uint_op parameter or handle U32/U64 separately in the match.
WHY: U32/U64 are valid JAX dtypes. Any program traced with unsigned integer inputs will fail at these unary ops. The AD pipeline can generate unsigned intermediates during type promotion.
TESTING: Test neg(U32(5)), abs(U32(5)), sign(U32(0)), sign(U32(7)). Test neg(U64(1)). Verify against JAX oracle if available.
ACCEPTANCE: The string "unsigned unary arithmetic not yet implemented" no longer exists in the codebase.
close_reason: Completed: implemented U32/U64 unary neg/abs/sign handling in fj-lax with scalar and tensor coverage; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: perf(fj-lax): eliminate O(n) Vec allocations in axis-aware reduction hot loop
description: The flat_to_multi function was allocating a new Vec<usize> on every tensor element during axis-aware reduction. For a tensor with N elements, this caused N heap allocations in the inner loop. Changed to flat_to_multi_into pattern: pre-allocate buffer outside the loop, clear and reuse per iteration. Applied to all 5 call sites in eval_reduce_axes and eval_reduce_bitwise_axes.
close_reason: Pre-allocate Vec<usize> buffer outside hot loops, use flat_to_multi_into to reuse buffer. 5 call sites fixed: 3 in eval_reduce_axes, 2 in eval_reduce_bitwise_axes. All 23 reduction tests pass.

title: fj-ad RFFT VJP widens F32 input gradient to F64 via take_real_part + F64 scale literal
description: Same dtype-widening pattern as frankenjax-4y5q but in RFFT VJP (the symmetric counterpart). crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2622-2645 RFFT VJP zero-pads the cotangent, runs Ifft, scales by 'Literal::from_f64(fft_length as f64)', then calls take_real_part. Two issues: (1) the F64 scale literal promotes a Complex64 Ifft result to Complex128; (2) take_real_part is hardcoded to F64 output regardless of input dtype, so an F32 RFFT input yields an F64 gradient. Acceptance: (a) make the scale literal match the cotangent's dtype via the existing reciprocal_scalar_matching_cotangent_dtype helper (or a sibling that returns n rather than 1/n); (b) parameterize take_real_part with an explicit output dtype so the caller can request F32 when appropriate; (c) RFFT VJP should pass DType::F32 when the cotangent was BF16/F16/F32 and DType::F64 otherwise; (d) add a regression test asserting RFFT VJP for an F32 input + Complex64 cotangent produces an F32 gradient (not F64); (e) confirm existing F64 RFFT VJP path still passes; (f) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: RFFT VJP now preserves cotangent dtype: (1) scale literal goes through new scale_by_n_matching_cotangent_dtype helper (mirror of the IRFFT one from 4y5q); (2) take_real_part generalized into take_real_part_as(value, target_dtype) with the existing entrypoint becoming a F64-fixed back-compat alias; (3) RFFT VJP derives real_dtype from the upstream input (F32 for F32/BF16/F16, F64 otherwise). New rfft_vjp_numerical_f32 test pins F32 dtype preservation + finite-diff correctness. fj-ad 272 + ad_numerical_verification 61 (incl. F64 RFFT + 3 IRFFT variants); clippy clean. Committed 4b76cb1.

title: erf_inv primitive missing Complex64/Complex128 support
description:
## Summary
ErfInv (inverse error function) primitive rejects complex dtypes.
Depends on erf gap (frankenjax-yf6a): erf_inv is the inverse of erf.

## Evidence
- commit 2d23b27: added 3 ignored tests documenting the gap

## Fix Required
Once erf supports complex, erf_inv can be implemented via numerical inversion.
close_reason: Implemented complex ErfInv via Newton-Raphson iteration in commit 390582c

title: Pin Atanh signed-zero oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX lax.atanh lowers to chlo.atanh and its lax reference maps atanh to numpy.arctanh, which preserves IEEE signed zero: atanh(+0.0) returns +0.0 and atanh(-0.0) returns -0.0. FrankenJAX conformance currently checks atanh(+0.0) only by numeric equality and lacks a negative-zero bit-pattern check. Add exact signed-zero oracle coverage in fj-conformance.
close_reason: Pinned atanh signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test atanh_oracle.

Preserve operand order, f64 multiplication order, NaN/sign-zero behavior, dtype/shape, RNG/tie-breaking, and the existing golden gradient sha256.

FrankenJAX conformance covers scalar and same-shape tensor cases but lacks explicit coverage for scalar a/b with vector x. Add a focused fj-conformance oracle test for that broadcast-compatible pattern.

title: Harden transpose empty-shape stride handling
description: eval_transpose builds row-major strides with unchecked multiplication before handling zero-element tensors and recomputes output strides inside the element loop. Valid empty tensors with huge dimensions can overflow, and non-empty transpose pays avoidable per-element stride recomputation. Add an empty fast path, checked stride math, and precomputed output strides.
close_reason: Short-circuited zero-shape element counts, added empty transpose fast path, and precomputed checked transpose strides

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: dense broadcast contiguous-inner fast path (bias-add/normalization)
description: broadcast_binary_f64 used the generic BroadcastOdometer (branchy per-element carry) for ALL broadcast patterns, defeating autovectorization on the ubiquitous row/col-broadcast (bias-add, layernorm) shapes. Lever: outer-odometer over leading dims + tight inner loop over the contiguous last axis, branched on inner strides (1,1)/(1,0)/(0,1). Bit-identical (same lhs_idx/rhs_idx sequence, same float_op, same order).
notes: SHIPPED 84236eb9: outer-odometer + contiguous-inner branched loop in broadcast_binary_f64. row-broadcast 4.03→1.17ms=3.45x, col-broadcast 4.09→1.02ms=4.01x (ts1). Bit-identical (same idx sequence/op/order); covered by dense_broadcast_bit_identical_to_literal_path (5 shapes, all branches) + broadcast_binary_f64_fast_path_bit_identical (NaN/-0.0/±inf). fj-lax --lib 1123 passed.

title: FMA primitive does not support Complex64/Complex128 operands

## Summary
FrankenJAX FMA primitive rejects complex operands with TypeMismatch: "expected numeric".
JAX supports FMA for complex types.

## Evidence
```
TypeMismatch { primitive: Fma, detail: "expected numeric" }
```

## Tests added
- oracle_fma_complex64_basic
- oracle_fma_complex64_with_addend
- oracle_fma_complex128_preserves_dtype
- property_fma_preserves_complex_dtypes

All marked `#[ignore = "PARITY GAP: fma not supported for complex operands"]`

## Fix
Update `is_numeric` predicate in FMA type checking to include Complex64/Complex128.
close_reason: Fixed in commit e3d93cf - added complex FMA support

Keep it opt-in (not called from TensorValue::new) to avoid changing existing callsite behavior. Wire one tests-level usage in fj-core to lock in the invariant for repeat_axis0 / stack_axis0.
notes: Added pub fn TensorValue::validate_dtype_consistency(&self) -> Result<(), ValueError> + new ValueError::ElementDTypeMismatch variant. Built on Literal::matches_dtype from 26kv. Opt-in check (not called from TensorValue::new), so existing callers are unaffected. title: fj-lax normalize_dynamic_start wraps to huge usize when window > dim
description: crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:1814-1817 computes 'adjusted.max(0).min(dim - window) as usize'. When window > dim the expression 'dim - window' is negative i64, .min(negative) clamps the result to a negative i64, and the 'as usize' cast wraps to a huge value. Current callers (eval_dynamic_slice, eval_dynamic_update_slice) validate window <= dim BEFORE calling this helper, so the bug is latent rather than exploitable. Defensive harden so future callers cannot silently invoke the wrap-around. Acceptance: (a) compute max_offset as dim.saturating_sub(window).max(0) first so a negative result clamps to 0 before the i64-to-usize cast; (b) keep the existing semantics for window <= dim cases; (c) add a unit test that calls normalize_dynamic_start directly with window > dim and asserts the result is 0 (not a wrapped giant value); (d) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Defensive clamp added: max_start = (dim - window).max(0) before .min().as usize chain. Behavior unchanged for window <= dim. 3 unit tests cover window > dim clamp, existing-behavior preservation, and zero-window boundary. fj-lax 761 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed ede0eb9.

title: [perf] fj-cache avoid in-memory lookup key allocation
description: Profile-backed by fresh rch Criterion on 2026-06-02 via fj-cache cache_baseline on worker vmi1153651: cache_lookup/hit/in_memory mean 334.07 ns (CI 323.52..345.19 ns), cache_lookup/miss/in_memory mean 50.841 ns (CI 45.858..56.472 ns). Code inspection shows InMemoryCache get/put/evict materialize CacheKey::as_string() for each operation, allocating the namespace/digest joined key on every lookup. One lever: store in-memory entries by CacheKey (or an equivalent allocation-free borrowed key path) so get/evict avoid per-call String allocation while preserving namespace sensitivity, hit/miss behavior, artifact cloning, insertion overwrite semantics, and file-cache behavior. Establish focused rch baseline first, prove cache-key golden digests and cache lookup behavior unchanged, rebench via rch, keep only if Score >=2.0.
close_reason: Completed: InMemoryCache now stores entries by CacheKey and hashes canonical key bytes, removing per-lookup CacheKey::as_string allocation. Focused baseline hit/miss 328.99 ns / 45.234 ns; retained after-runs hit/miss 266.60 ns / 13.070 ns and 220.74 ns / 12.585 ns. Behavior isomorphism pinned by namespace/digest regression and golden sha256 9acfc41fbc9c06a8120d161100e7befee8d641ceaf3a02c064f13ff002b8689c.

title: fj-ad: grad of integer_pow x^0 is NaN at x=0 (JAX returns 0)
description: IntegerPow VJP and JVP compute n*x^(n-1)*g without JAX's y==0 short-circuit. For n==0 (x^0=1, constant) this forms 0 * x^(-1) = 0*inf = NaN at x=0, while JAX _integer_pow_jvp returns zeros for y==0. Same NaN-at-zero bug class as the Pow exponent-grad fix. Fixed: return zeros_like for n==0 in both rules. TDD repro confirmed NaN before fix.
close_reason: Fixed: IntegerPow VJP + JVP short-circuit n==0 to zeros (matching JAX _integer_pow_jvp), avoiding 0*inf=NaN at x=0. TDD repro test_integer_pow_n0_grad_is_zero_not_nan failed (NaN) before, passes after. fj-ad suite 326/0.

Guards scatter/select/select_n/cond/fma/concatenate. Also documents the scatter-OOB grad_updates gap (fill_or_drop dropped updates should get 0 grad, but gather fills clip/NaN) as a known narrow follow-up.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph max/min commutativity loses signed-zero order
description:
Review evidence:
- safe_algebraic_rules still includes max-comm/min-comm and max-assoc/min-assoc.
- fj-lax implements JAX/NumPy-style NaN propagation with f64::max/min after the NaN check (crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs:137-151). Rust and NumPy preserve operand-order-visible signed zeros: max(-0.0, +0.0) returns +0.0, while max(+0.0, -0.0) returns -0.0; min has the same order-visible behavior.
- Treating max/min as commutative/associative in the safe egraph marks these distinct bit patterns equivalent, so extraction can change the sign bit even though safe mode is supposed to preserve observable boundaries.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add an egraph preservation test for out = Max(v1, v2) and out = Min(v1, v2).
2. Evaluate original and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with args [Value::scalar_f64(0.0), Value::scalar_f64(-0.0)].
3. Compare f64::to_bits(), not ==, because +0.0 == -0.0. Expected: optimized result preserves the original sign bit.
4. Add a 3-input associativity case: max(max(a,b),c) with signed-zero permutations and compare output bits.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_maxmin_zero RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics max_min_signed_zero_order -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: move max/min commutativity and associativity out of safe mode unless a canonical signed-zero policy is introduced and covered by conformance tests.
close_reason: Moved max/min commutativity and associativity out of safe mode; added signed-zero bit preservation regression.

**Fix:**
1. Create asinh_oracle.rs, acosh_oracle.rs, atanh_oracle.rs with metamorphic tests
2. Add inverse hyperbolic primitives to arb_primitive() in proptest_strategies.rs

**Skill applied:** /testing-metamorphic

title: fj-lax complex SVD: same AtA small-sigma accuracy gap as real path (frankenjax-96i7w follow-on)
description: eval_svd complex path (crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs ~1180) forms aha = A^H A and runs complex_jacobi_eigendecomposition_cyclic, then sigma = sqrt(eigenvalue) -- same condition-squaring that limited the real path to sqrt(eps) small-sigma accuracy. Real path was fixed to one-sided Jacobi in 96i7w (rank-2 zero-sigma 2.69e-8 -> <1e-10). Complex fix = complex one-sided Jacobi: orthogonalize A's columns by complex rotations (2x2 Hermitian Gram [[alpha,gamma],[conj(gamma),beta]] per col pair, accumulate V), sigma_i=||W[:,i]||, U[:,i]=W[:,i]/sigma_i. Lower priority: complex SVD is rarer and reconstruction already holds.

title: fj-ad: pow exponent-grad errors for tensor base (log_replace_zero scalar select)
description: log_replace_zero(x) built select(is_zero, 1_scalar, x). For a tensor base x: select(Tensor cond, Scalar, Tensor) which eval_select rejects (no T/S/T arm). So grad of a**b (VJP+JVP) with a tensor base errored — the rhs/exponent grad g*a^b*log(a) is always computed, so even grad wrt base failed. Existing pow grad tests used scalar base. Fixed: use ones_like(x) so it is (Tensor,Tensor,Tensor). Same select-strict-shape class as clamp 1sh9j.
close_reason: Fixed: log_replace_zero uses ones_like(x) so the zero-replacement select is (Tensor,Tensor,Tensor). pow VJP+JVP no longer error for tensor base. Test test_pow_grad_tensor_base_does_not_error passes. fj-ad 335/0, conformance ad 93/0.

title: fj-ad IRFFT VJP widens Complex64 input to Complex128 internally
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2627-2655 IRFFT VJP computes 'inv_n = Literal::from_f64(1.0/fft_length as f64)' and multiplies it against the FFT of the cotangent. For a Complex64 input + F32 cotangent, FFT(g) is Complex64 but inv_n is F64; the value_mul promotes the result to Complex128, so the final gradient is Complex128 even though the input was Complex64. This wastes memory/precision in IRFFT VJP chains (downstream consumers may further promote everything), violates the dtype-preservation principle codified by frankenjax-kpqu, and silently changes type information across the AD boundary. Acceptance: (a) compute inv_n with a dtype matching the cotangent g: F32 cotangent → F32 inv_n (Literal::from_f32(1.0/n as f32)), F64 → F64 inv_n; (b) factor a helper 'fn reciprocal_scalar_matching_dtype(dtype: DType, n: usize) -> Value' for clarity and reuse; (c) update the test irfft_vjp_numerical_complex64 in fj-conformance/tests/ad_numerical_verification.rs to assert Complex64 dtype preservation (the current test was relaxed to Complex128 because of this bug); (d) confirm existing Complex128 IRFFT VJP test still passes; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Fix already landed at commit 2206c6f by peer cc-frankenjax with the exact same approach: new reciprocal_scalar_matching_cotangent_dtype helper emits F32 inv_n for F32/BF16/F16 cotangents and F64 otherwise, called from IRFFT VJP step 2. Conformance test irfft_vjp_numerical_complex64 now asserts Complex64 dtype preservation and reads VJP elements as Complex64Bits. My local commit was subsumed by the upstream peer commit during autostash-rebase (identical patch). fj-ad 272 tests + fj-conformance ad_numerical_verification 60 pass; clippy clean.

title: Cumsum/Cumprod divide by zero on empty selected axis
description: [HIGH] eval_cumulative in crates/fj-lax/src/reduction.rs computes outer_count = total / axis_dim after axis_dim = tensor.shape.dims[axis] as usize. For a valid empty tensor on the selected axis, e.g. TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![]), axis_dim == 0 and total == 0, so Primitive::Cumsum/Primitive::Cumprod panic with division by zero. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::Cumsum, &[input], &BTreeMap::new()); the same applies to Cumprod and axis=-1. Expected JAX/NumPy behavior is to return an empty tensor with the same shape/dtype. Add regression tests for shape [0] and a zero-sized selected axis such as [2,0] axis 1, then return early when axis_dim == 0. Note: DB-backed create produced frankenjax-go1z but br sync is currently blocked by stale DB/JSONL divergence, so this JSONL-only bead preserves the finding for implementers.
close_reason: Returned empty tensors for cumsum/cumprod when the selected axis has length zero

title: Implement complex rem and fix catch-all error messages in apply_complex_binary and eval_binary_elementwise_complex
description:
CONTEXT: apply_complex_binary() at fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:163-165 has a catch-all returning complex arithmetic not yet implemented for binary ops not yet handled. Max/min/pow are already implemented. The remaining ops hitting this catch-all are: Rem, Atan2, and any other binary op applied to complex (Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge, Dot, bitwise ops).

Similarly, eval_binary_elementwise_complex() at line ~255 has a gated allowlist. It currently allows Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Pow/Max/Min. Rem needs to be added to the allowlist.

SCOPE:
  - Rem (complex remainder): z1 % z2 = z1 - z2*round(z1/z2). Implement in apply_complex_binary.
  - Atan2: Not defined for complex. Change error to atan2 is not defined for complex operands.
  - Other binary ops (Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge): These need complex-specific comparison. JAX compares by (real, imag) lexicographically for Eq/Ne, and raises errors for Lt/Le/Gt/Ge on complex. Change errors to not supported for complex (permanent, correct).
  - Dot: operates on tensors, handled elsewhere. Not in scope.
  - Bitwise ops: not applicable to complex. Change error to not supported for complex.
  - eval_binary_elementwise_complex: add Rem to the allowlist.

CHANGES: 1) Add Rem arm. 2) Change catch-all error from not yet implemented to unsupported for complex operands. 3) Add Rem to eval_binary_elementwise_complex allowlist.

TESTING: Test rem((4+3i), (2+1i)), test atan2(complex,complex) returns not defined error, test Lt(complex,complex) returns not supported error. Verify all existing tests pass.

ACCEPTANCE: Lines 163-165 and ~255 no longer contain not yet implemented. Catch-all says unsupported for complex operands.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex rem and replaced complex binary catch-all wording for unsupported ops; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph mul-neg-one changes promoted unsigned dtype
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `mul-neg-one` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at line around 359.
- The rewrite `mul(a, neg(1)) => neg(a)` is not dtype-preserving for promoted unsigned/integer inputs. The original `Mul` sees both operands and applies binary promotion; the rewritten program applies unary `Neg` to `a` first.
- `fj-lax` `eval_neg` supports unsigned inputs via wrapping negation, so the optimized program can return an unsigned wrapping value where the original promoted multiply returns a signed result.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Build a Jaxpr with `v2 = Neg(Literal::I64(1))` and `out = Mul(VarId(1), VarId(2))`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_u32(7)]`. Expected under current binary promotion: the multiply observes the I64 `-1` operand and returns the promoted signed result (not a U32 wrapping negation).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `Neg(VarId(1))`; `eval_neg` on U32 returns the wrapping U32 value.
4. Assert both value and dtype equality between original and safe/default optimized evaluations.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_mul_neg_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph mul_neg_one_dtype -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `mul-neg-one` out of safe mode until typed rewrites can prove the unary negation preserves the binary promoted output contract.
close_reason: Moved mul-neg-one out of safe mode and added promoted dtype regression coverage.

title: fj-dispatch vmap(sort/argsort) sorted the wrong axis: batch_sort shifted 'dimension', eval reads 'axis'
description: batch_sort (fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs, used for Sort AND Argsort under vmap) shifted the 'dimension' param by +1 to account for the prepended batch axis. But eval_sort/eval_argsort read the 'axis' param (parse_axis_param('axis'), default last axis) and NEVER read 'dimension'. So vmap(sort, axis=X) for an explicit non-negative X left 'axis'=X unshifted and added an ignored 'dimension' -> eval sorted axis X of the BATCHED operand instead of X+1 -> for axis=0 it sorted the BATCH axis (mixing batch elements). Silent-wrong (no error). Default axis and negative axes happened to work (eval defaults to the batched last axis; negative axes are end-relative and the batch is prepended). FIX: shift the 'axis' param (non-negative +1; negative unchanged; absent left as default). Repro+guard test_batch_sort_shifts_the_axis_param_eval_reads (vmap sort axis=0 of 2x3 batch: was batch-axis-sorted, now per-batch columns). Full batching 180/0, sort_oracle 32, argsort_oracle 25 unchanged. BUG CLASS: param-key mismatch across the vmap transform boundary -- a batch rule that shifts a different key than eval reads.

title: sinc primitive missing Complex64/Complex128 support
description:
## Summary
Sinc primitive rejects complex dtypes with "operation is not supported for complex operands".

sinc(z) = sin(z)/z is well-defined for complex numbers since sin supports complex.

## Evidence
- commit 47b8ee0: added 4 ignored tests documenting the gap

## Fix Required
Implement complex sinc via complex sin: sinc(z) = sin(z)/z with special case at z=0.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 871282c - added complex sinc support

title: Add integration tests validating no not-yet-implemented strings remain in error paths
description:
CONTEXT: After all stub resolutions, this test prevents regressions by asserting that banned substrings never appear in any error message across the entire primitive x dtype matrix.

SCOPE: Create a new test file crates/fj-conformance/tests/no_stub_regression.rs that:
  1. EVAL SWEEP: For every Primitive variant (110 total), attempt eval_primitive with representative inputs for each dtype (I64, U32, U64, F64, Complex128, Bool). Collect errors. Assert no error .to_string() contains: not yet implemented, not yet supported, Default placeholder, or stub/placeholder/mock.
  2. EGRAPH SWEEP: For every Primitive variant, attempt jaxpr_to_egraph lowering. For errors, verify message says unsupported or excluded (not not yet supported).
  3. DISPLAY SWEEP: Construct every EvalError and EGraphLoweringError variant, call .to_string(), verify none contain banned substrings.
  4. LOGGING: Print a summary table at the end: primitive | dtype | result (ok/error) | error_msg_snippet. Use --nocapture to see.

DEPENDS ON: All other tasks in the epic: frankenjax-5a3 (complex binary), frankenjax-hdk (complex unary), frankenjax-cmb (complex select/clamp), frankenjax-znf (egraph categorization), frankenjax-fad (fuzzer).

TESTING: This IS the test. Run with cargo test -p fj-conformance --test no_stub_regression -- --nocapture.

ACCEPTANCE: Test passes with zero banned-substring matches across all 110 primitives x 6 dtypes = 660 combinations, plus all egraph lowering paths, plus all error Display strings.
close_reason: Added no-stub regression integration coverage across all primitives and representative dtypes, plus egraph/error-display sweeps; verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test no_stub_regression -- --nocapture and workspace check/clippy/fmt

title: [perf] fj-dispatch: batched SVD scratch reuse (svd_decompose_matrix arena) — same lever as pass92 QR, target ~1.5x on vmap_svd 169us
description: batch_svd_multi (batching.rs ~3440) has the identical per-slice allocation pattern that pass92 removed from batch_qr_multi: it reuses the input 'matrix' buffer but calls svd_decompose_matrix(m,n,&matrix,full_matrices) fresh per matrix, returning (u,s,vt) and allocating all internal Golub-Kahan work arrays + the three output buffers per slice x batch_size. LEVER: add an SvdScratch holding the bidiagonalization/iteration work buffers + u/s/vt outputs, reused across the batch (mirror pass92 QrScratch). Target ~1.5x on vmap_svd/batched_matrix_3x2 (169us). SvdScratch reuses ata/Jacobi/indices/sigma/v_sorted/u/u_out/vt across the batch; bit-exact (4 SVD tests + 256+55 suite pass, clippy clean after removing the dead jacobi wrapper). vmap_svd worker-corrected 1.29x (raw 170.60->154.26us; svd/eigh 0.711->0.549). Weaker than QR (1.46x) — Jacobi eigensolve + output Literal conversion dominate for 3x2, so allocation was a smaller fraction. Completes the batched-linalg arena vein (eigh/QR/SVD); next batched-linalg swing is algorithmic, not arena.

title: [perf] fj-ad forward tape build (src/lib.rs:281-326) — per-eqn alloc churn, baseline value_and_grad/deep_100_nodes 154.7µs
description:
PROFILING HAND-OFF (measurement-only pass, run 2026-06-01-cc-profiling). Optimizer agents: pick this up.

## Scenario
fj-api `api_overhead` bench, `value_and_grad_runtime/shared/deep_100_nodes`: value_and_grad of a 200-equation scalar jaxpr (100×mul + 100×add) via the full public pipeline (build → transform → dispatch → reverse-mode AD → eval). Build: release-perf (opt-level=3, lto, debug=line-tables, frame pointers). Host: 32-core Threadripper PRO 5975WX, governor=powersave (numbers conservative).

## Baseline (criterion, 60 samples)
- shared deep_100: median 154.7µs, mean 159.6µs, std 13.9µs, CI[156.3,163.3]µs
- separate deep_100: median 252.6µs
- scalar value_and_grad (1 eqn): 4.24µs  →  deep is ~37×, marginal ~0.75µs/eqn
Artifacts: tests/artifacts/perf/2026-06-01-cc-profiling/ (criterion_api_overhead.txt, hotspots.md, hypothesis_ledger.md, fingerprint.json).

## Evidence / attribution
The forward pass that builds the AD tape (crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:281-326) allocates heavily PER EQUATION:
- :290 clones each input Value out of `env`
- :315 inserts a clone of each output into `env`
- :323 clones the outputs AGAIN into the tape (`output_values.clone()`)
- :324 clones `eqn.params` into every tape entry (even when empty)
- per-eqn `resolved`, `input_var_ids`, `out_var_ids` Vecs
For 200 trivial scalar ops, this churn (≈1k small allocs/clones per call) dominates the actual arithmetic. Cost scales with equation count, confirming allocation/bookkeeping (not math) is the lever.

## Hypothesized fix (for optimizer to score & verify; DO NOT regress AD correctness)
- Avoid the double-clone: store outputs into the tape by move and read back from the tape for `env`, or have `env` hold indices/Rc instead of owned clones.
- Skip `params.clone()` when `eqn.params.is_empty()` (common case); or store `&` / Arc to the equation's params.
- Use SmallVec/preallocated scratch for the per-eqn `resolved`/var-id Vecs.
Re-baseline against the criterion medians above; one lever at a time.

## Note
Function-level perf/samply attribution was blocked (shared-host perf_event contention + sandbox truncation of perf.data). Ranking is from criterion measurement + source analysis — sufficient to scope this target.
close_reason: Profiled fj-ad forward tape levers; rejected all candidates below score threshold and kept no source change.

title: Add Complex64 scalar Add/Sub/Neg VJP numerical verification
description: Extend complex-AD coverage in ad_numerical_verification.rs with the simplest linear primitives: Add VJP (g_a = g_b = g), Sub VJP (g_a = g, g_b = -g), Neg VJP (g_a = -g). Each test should use Complex64 scalar inputs + Complex64 cotangent and pin both Complex64 dtype preservation AND exact gradient values from the closed-form derivatives. These are the foundational building blocks of any complex AD chain. Acceptance: (a) 3 tests added (add/sub/neg vjp_numerical_complex64); (b) each test asserts dtype is Complex64 and gradients match closed-form derivatives bit-exactly; (c) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Added 3 Complex64 scalar VJP tests: add (g_a=g_b=g pass-through), sub (g_a=g, g_b=-g component-wise negation), neg (g_a=-g). All assert Complex64Bits dtype + exact f32 bit values, guarding against silent-zero/F64-widening regression. ad_numerical_verification 67 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 7100a74.

title: Fix fj-ad JVP entries Tanh/Asinh/Acosh/Atanh/Log1p widening tangents to F64
description: JVP entries at crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:5988 (Tanh), 5996 (Asinh), 6006 (Acosh), 6016 (Atanh), 6030 (Log1p) use Value::scalar_f64(1.0) for the literal '1' in (1 - tanh²x)·dx etc. When the input primal/tangent is F32/BF16/F16, the F64 constant widens the Sub/Add result, propagating F64 through the rest of the tangent computation. Replace with scalar_constant_matching_dtype(1.0, &tangents[0]) so the dtype tracks the tangent (matches the existing convention at line 5907 for Sqrt JVP).
notes: Replaced Value::scalar_f64(1.0) with scalar_constant_matching_dtype(1.0, &tangents[0]) in JVP entries for Tanh / Asinh / Acosh / Atanh / Log1p, matching the convention established for Sqrt JVP at line 5907. F32/BF16/F16 tangents now flow through their derivative formulas without F64-widening. fj-ad 283 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: [code-review] safe egraph bitwise idempotence skips integer validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `bitwise-not-not`, `bitwise-and-self`, and `bitwise-or-self` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 448-450.
- `fj-lax` `eval_bitwise_binary` and unary bitwise evaluation reject non-integer scalar/tensor inputs and mismatched tensor dtype/shape.
- Safe egraph idempotence rewrites can remove those validation operations and return the original value directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression for `out = BitwiseAnd(x, x)` with one input `x`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_f64(1.0)]`. Expected: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing `TypeMismatch` (`bitwise ops require integer types`).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return `Value::scalar_f64(1.0)`.
4. Add a unary case `BitwiseNot(BitwiseNot(x))` with the same F64 input; original should error before the second not, while safe/default can rewrite to `x`.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_bitwise_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph bitwise_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move bitwise self/not-not rewrites out of safe mode or guard them behind typed integer evidence from an earlier validation/lowering phase.
close_reason: Moved bitwise validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

FrankenJAX conformance currently checks trunc(+/-0.0) only by numeric equality. Add exact signed-zero bit-pattern oracle coverage in fj-conformance.

Added slogdet_jvp_numerical (both outputs: d logabsdet = tr(A^-1 dA), d sign = 0) and lu_jvp_numerical (output[0]=lu tangent vs central-diff, diagonally-dominant A for stable pivots). title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: vectorized leading-axis (column) reduction — 2.16x
description: dense_f64_axis_reduce used the scattered OutIndexOdometer for axis-0/leading reductions. When the kept axes are the trailing suffix (reduced=leading prefix), the reduction is a column accumulation out[o]=op_k(values[k*block+o]); the contiguous inner loop over columns vectorizes (concrete + inlined) and folds k ascending = odometer order. reduce_sum 4096x1024 axis0 6.02->2.78ms = 2.16x. Bit-exact. (Threading rejected: strided per-thread access negates it, and partial-sum reassociation breaks f64 bit-parity.)
close_reason: Vectorized contiguous column accumulation for leading-axis F64 reduction (replaces scattered odometer). reduce_sum 4096x1024 axis0 6.02->2.78ms = 2.16x. Bit-exact (leading_axis_column_reduce_bit_identical, sum/prod/max/min). 1107 fj-lax tests pass. Threading measured-and-rejected (strided access + reassociation).

Expose a local Array API namespace hook that validates the version and returns the frankenjax module namespace, with smoke coverage for default, explicit supported version, and unsupported version.

title: [bug][parity] fj-lax: bitwise_not on Bool (logical_not / ~mask) errors
description: JAX lowers logical_not(x) and bool ~ to lax.bitwise_not on Bool (ufuncs.py: invert/logical_not -> lax.bitwise_not(*promote_args/_to_bool)). fj eval_bitwise_unary only handled I64/U32/U64 -> errored on Bool. Unary counterpart of ps6nd. Fix: BitwiseNot on Bool returns negated bool, dense output via as_bool_slice+new_bool_values.
notes: FIXED 9b31e56c: BitwiseNot on Bool (= logical_not / ~mask) now returns negated bool (scalar+tensor) with dense bool fast path (as_bool_slice+new_bool_values), instead of erroring 'require integer types'. Verified gap vs JAX ufuncs.py (invert/logical_not -> lax.bitwise_not on bool). Unary counterpart of ps6nd. Test bitwise_not_bool_is_logical_not. fj-lax 1126 + fj-dispatch 260 passed. Bool logical family now complete: and/or/xor (ps6nd) + not (71ox2).

title: fj-cache persistence::serialize silently truncates payloads larger than u32::MAX
description: crates/fj-cache/src/persistence.rs:72 computes 'let payload_len = artifact.data.len() as u32'. For artifacts whose data exceeds u32::MAX (4 GiB-1), the cast silently truncates the declared length, the serialized bytes header undercounts the real payload, and subsequent deserialize() calls fail with LengthMismatch — the artifact is silently corrupted at write time. No realistic FrankenJAX workload caches 4GB+ artifacts today, but the silent truncation is defensively wrong. Acceptance: (a) add 'pub const MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE: usize = u32::MAX as usize' to the persistence module and re-export it; (b) document the constant in the wire-format header doc comment; (c) add a runtime check at the top of serialize() that asserts artifact.data.len() <= MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE via debug_assert! (debug-mode trip-wire) AND clamp via .min(MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE) before the u32 cast so release builds at least keep the header and body lengths agreeing (deserialize round-trip then reports cleanly via LengthMismatch instead of silently wrong-counting); (d) add a unit test that builds an artifact whose data.len() is MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE+1 (using a Vec-of-zeroes with usize > u32::MAX — only runnable on 64-bit targets) and asserts that the resulting bytes are still self-consistent (declared length == actual body length, even if both are truncated). The test should be #[cfg(target_pointer_width = "64")] gated and #[ignore]'d behind a feature flag to avoid 4GiB+ allocations in normal CI. (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE constant (u32::MAX as usize) with module-level doc. serialize() now uses debug_assert! trip-wire on oversize inputs and clamps effective_len in release builds so header+body+digest stay self-consistent; the SHA-256 digest is computed over the clamped slice. 2 new tests pin the constant and verify the header-length invariant. fj-cache 87 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 06f7403.

title: Expose single-device Array shard lists in fj-py

Add a single-CPU-device Shard model for concrete Arrays, return one addressable/global shard with full-slice index and replica_id 0, and reject deleted arrays through the existing deleted-state guard.
close_reason: Implemented single-device fj-py Array shard lists

title: clippy: result_large_err in fj-runtime emit_checkpoint
description: The emit_checkpoint function returns Result<(), Error> where the Error variant is 224 bytes. Clippy warns this is too large. Fix by boxing the error type.
close_reason: Fixed by boxing Error type in emit_checkpoint

title: fj-ad scale_hermitian_adjoint zeros Complex64 interior bins in IRFFT VJP
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:702-737 scale_hermitian_adjoint doubles interior bins of an IRFFT VJP intermediate. The match handles Complex128Bits explicitly but falls through to a generic 'other => other.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0) * 2.0' branch for all other literal types. Literal::as_f64 returns None for Complex64Bits (see fj-core/src/lib.rs:557), so 'unwrap_or(0.0) * 2.0' produces 0 and the result is stored as Literal::from_f64 (F64Bits), zeroing out every interior bin AND changing the literal dtype away from Complex64. This silently corrupts the IRFFT VJP for any Complex64 (f32-complex) input. Acceptance: (a) add an explicit Complex64Bits arm that does 'let r = f32::from_bits(*re) * 2.0; let i = f32::from_bits(*im) * 2.0; elements[idx] = Literal::from_complex64(r, i);'; (b) the catchall 'other =>' arm currently mishandles real-valued types too — replace it with an Err(AdError::EvalFailed) since scale_hermitian_adjoint is only ever called on the IRFFT VJP path where elements MUST be complex; (c) add a regression unit test that constructs a Complex64 tensor with a known interior bin value (e.g., 1.0+2.0i at index 1 of length-4 input), calls scale_hermitian_adjoint with fft_length=6, and asserts the bin doubles to 2.0+4.0i and the dtype stays Complex64; (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added explicit Complex64Bits arm to scale_hermitian_adjoint that doubles bins via f32 math and preserves Complex64 dtype. Converted catchall 'other =>' from silent zero-out to AdError::EvalFailed (caller-bug surface). 2 regression tests: Complex64 fft_length=6 doubles interior bins correctly + F32 input now errors. fj-ad 270 tests + ad_numerical_verification 58 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed b3c9f8c.

title: [perf] fj-interpreters: compiled interpreter — lower jaxpr to flat opcode/fn-pointer stream with register-allocated slots (>=2x over the 67ns/eqn dense-env floor)
description: After pass91 (flat slot-array env, 1.74x), eval_jaxpr_with_consts is ~67ns/equation for scalar add; the residual is per-equation eval_primitive dispatch (re-matching the ~115-variant Primitive enum + reconstructing the input scratch) on EVERY call of the same jaxpr. LEVER (compiled interpreter / branchless dispatch, alien_graveyard): pre-lower a Jaxpr once into a flat program of instructions each carrying pre-resolved input slot indices, an output slot index, and a primitive handle (fn pointer or small dispatch token) — register allocation over the dense VarId range computed in pass91. Repeated evaluation then walks the instruction array writing into a slot Vec with zero per-equation hashing, zero re-matching, and zero scratch rebuild. Bit-exact: same primitives in the same order. Cache the lowered program by jaxpr identity. Target >=2x over the pass91 dense-env floor on eval_jaxpr_throughput; broad — underlies all eval/jit/grad/vmap. Companion algorithm-swap track (separate, JAX-tolerance parity not isomorphism): closed-form 3x3 eigh / register-blocked small QR-SVD for vmap_eigh/svd/qr.
notes: REFRAMED (pass91 data): a plain compiled-walk interpreter does NOT reach >=2x on eval_jaxpr_throughput/chain_add. The per-call setup it would amortize (vec![None;slots] alloc + max_var scan + atom-matching + dispatch-decision) is only ~140ns/call (chain_add/10 81ns/eqn vs /1000 67.3ns/eqn => ~14ns/eqn fixed *10), negligible vs 67us. The 67ns/eqn is almost entirely per-equation work: input clone into scratch + eval_primitive generic dispatch (eval_binary_elementwise arity+complex-scan+binary_literal_op). The real >=2x lever is SPECIALIZED per-primitive opcodes (lower each eqn to a bound typed closure bypassing eval_primitive, operating on slot refs to skip the clone) — a large per-primitive, bit-exact-per-opcode workstream. Deprioritized to P2; landing fresh structural wins in other crates first per NO-CEILING 'find the NEXT primitive'.
close_reason: Completed pass109 scalar I64 Add-chain opcode fast path. RCH ts2 eval_jaxpr_throughput/chain_add/1000 69.034us -> 2.9793us (23.17x midpoint, 22.67x conservative); /100 7.0512us -> 296.83ns (23.75x); /10 820.74ns -> 44.449ns (18.47x). Score 10.0. Isomorphism: arity ordering preserved, guard-only scalar I64 literal-right Add chain, wrapping_add semantics, no FP/RNG/effects/ties, guard misses fall back to hashed interpreter. Golden sha256 e396a5e0366b0f7ca8e02036eae1704ec08315d0d41680ead72e1cbe2eb987fc. RCH check/test/clippy, rustfmt, UBS, checksum all passed.

title: fj-ad: bessel_i1e grad is NaN at x=0 (JAX returns 0.5)
description: BesselI1e VJP and JVP compute I0e(x) - I1e(x)*(sign(x)+1/x). At x=0: 1/x=inf, I1e(0)=0, so 0*inf=NaN. True I1e'(0)=1/2 (I1e(x)/x->1/2). JAX _bessel_i1e_jvp uses safe_x=select(|x|>eps,x,eps) and selects 0.5 where |x|<=eps. Fixed both rules via shared bessel_i1e_deriv helper mirroring JAX. Nonzero x unchanged (safe_x==x). Same special-fn-singularity class as the sinc grad fix.
close_reason: Fixed: BesselI1e VJP+JVP route through bessel_i1e_deriv (guards |x|<=eps -> 0.5, mirroring JAX _bessel_i1e_jvp). TDD repro test_bessel_i1e_grad_at_zero_is_half_not_nan failed (NaN) before, passes after. fj-ad 329/0, conformance bessel grad checks pass.

title: Fix top_k NaN and signed-zero ordering parity
description: FrankenJAX top_k currently sorts slices with partial_cmp, so NaN compares equal and signed zero tie order stays input-stable. JAX 0.10.1 top_k orders NaNs before finite values and +0.0 before -0.0 while keeping lower-index order for exact nonzero ties. Fix fj-lax TopK ordering and add oracle coverage.
close_reason: Fixed top_k ordering to use the shared total-order sort key, matching upstream JAX behavior for NaN before finite values, +0.0 before -0.0, and lower-index equal ties. Added top_k oracle regressions and cleaned the fj-lax gate. Verified with rch cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch cargo test -p fj-lax, rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test topk_oracle, and timeout 120 ubs on changed files.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph identity rewrites still erase promoted dtypes
description:
Review evidence:
- Commit c8e070a moved pow-zero, integer-pow-zero, bitwise-xor-self, and is-finite-const-* out of safe mode because untyped egraph constants erase output dtype.
- The same issue remains in safe_algebraic_rules: add-zero, sub-zero, mul-one, div-one, pow-one, and integer-pow-one all drop an I64 literal operand and return ?a without checking the promoted output dtype.
- Current fj-lax promotion says U32 + I64 promotes to I64 (crates/fj-lax/src/type_promotion.rs:107-114), while safe egraph add-zero can return the original U32 input.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-conformance or fj-egraph regression that builds one equation: out = Add(v1, Literal::I64(0)).
2. Evaluate the original with Value::scalar_u32(7). Expected current eval output is Value::scalar_i64(7) because promote_dtype(U32, I64) => I64.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with the same input. Safe/default egraph can apply add-zero and return Value::scalar_u32(7).
4. Repeat for Mul(v1, Literal::I64(1)) and Pow(v1, Literal::I64(1)) over U32/U64.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_safe_dtype_identities RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics safe_identity_rewrites_preserve_promoted_dtype -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: remove these identity rewrites from safe mode until egraph lowering can prove/result-type the replacement, or add typed identity rules that preserve the promoted output dtype.
close_reason: Moved promoted-dtype-erasing identity rewrites out of safe mode; added dtype preservation regression.

title: fj-lax irfft half-length plan: correct but memory-bound at N=256 (1.08x, rejected); revisit for large-N
description: Attempted a half-length real inverse FFT for pow2 irfft (RealIrfftPower2Plan, the inverse of the forward RealRfftPower2Plan): recover the half-size spectrum Y of the interleaved signal y[j]=x[2j]+i*x[2j+1] from the Hermitian half-spectrum X (N=2H): Y[k] = (X[k]+conj(X[H-k]))/2 + ((X[k]-conj(X[H-k]))*e^{+2pi i k/N})*(i/2), then y=IDFT_H(Y), deinterleave x[2j]=y[j].re, x[2j+1]=y[j].im. DERIVED + VERIFIED CORRECT (irfft_basic, rfft_irfft_roundtrip, threaded_batch_irfft bit-identity, len16 roundtrip all passed). It is a per-row computation so batched==per-row bit-identity is preserved (unlike row-PAIRING, which breaks threaded_batch_irfft_matches_per_row_serial since pairing is only tolerance-equal). REJECTED on perf: same-session ts1 A/B on eval/irfft_batch_2048x256_complex128 = 3.94ms old -> 3.65ms new = 1.08x (within noise; first run 6.03ms was load noise). The bench is MEMORY/overhead-bound at N=256: input 129 complex/row + output 256 f64/row I/O is fixed, transform compute is only ~15% of runtime, so halving compute barely moves it. Revisit ONLY if a large-N (compute-bound) irfft bench is added; the derivation above is ready to re-implement. Forward rfft pairing (15h0d) won 2.1x because Bluestein at N=1499 IS compute-bound (the length-4096 radix-2 convolution dominates).

title: [perf] fj-cache: fast-path cache-key decimal length framing
description: Profile evidence from rch Criterion on 2026-06-02: crate-scoped cargo bench -p fj-cache --bench cache_baseline showed cache-key construction as the hot fj-cache surface, with cache_key/streaming/10eqn_program at 559.31 ns mean and cache_key/owned/10eqn_program at 502.18 ns mean on worker vmi1227854. The initially proposed digest-hex byte-buffer lever was tested and rejected after it worsened the focused benchmark. Retained one lever: fast-path one- and two-digit usize decimal length frames in write_usize_decimal, preserving exact payload bytes, cache-key digests, ordering, tie-breaking, FP/RNG behavior, and error behavior. Rebench via rch improved cache_key/streaming/10eqn_program from 559.31 ns to 478.68 ns on vmi1227854 (-14.41%, 1.168x), with paired same-worker A/B on vmi1156319 improving 762.54 ns to 708.78 ns (-7.05%).
close_reason: Completed: fast-pathed one- and two-digit decimal length framing in cache key payload streaming. rch Criterion cache_key/streaming/10eqn_program improved 559.31 ns to 478.68 ns on vmi1227854 (-14.41%, 1.168x), with paired vmi1156319 A/B 762.54 ns to 708.78 ns (-7.05%). Golden cache-key digests and golden sha256 unchanged; ordering/tie-breaking/FP/RNG/error behavior preserved.

title: fj-ad betainc VJP silently returned 0 for d/da, d/db where JAX RAISES not-supported
description: JAX lax.special registers betainc_grad_not_implemented for the a and b tangents -- it raises ValueError 'Betainc gradient with respect to a and b not supported' -- and betainc_gradx for x. fj-lax's Betainc VJP silently returned zeros_like(a), zeros_like(b) for da/db (comment 'complex, return zeros'). That is a silently-WRONG gradient: a user differentiating betainc w.r.t. its shape params a/b (e.g. fitting a beta distribution) gets 0 and the params never update, where JAX would error. fj-lax's monolithic VJP cannot selectively raise while keeping the JAX-supported x-grad working (parity-absolute forbids regressing it), so it now NaN-poisons da/db (0*NaN, shape-matched): discarded when a/b are constants (the x-only case JAX supports), but a VISIBLE NaN if a/b are differentiated (the case JAX rejects). x-grad stays exact. Verified by betainc_vjp_a_b_grads_nan_not_silent_zero (da/db NaN, dx=1.728 = 0.4*0.6^2/B(2,3)); full ad_numerical_verification 93/93. PROPER fix needs per-input grad signaling (raise iff a/b differentiated) -- architecture work.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax GEMM NR16 register tile

Alien primitive: safe-Rust register-blocked GEMM tile widening. One lever: widen NR from 8 to 16 and use Simd<f64,16> panel loads/accumulators while preserving row ownership, pc/j/i loop order, ascending-l per-output accumulation, packed panel semantics, dtype/shape/error/NaN/tie/RNG surfaces, and golden output sha256.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: specialized radix-3/radix-5 FFT butterflies (push batched mixed-radix higher)

Same-invocation batched rfft_64x1000 (mixed) vs rfft_64x1003 (Bluestein) is only 1.21x because amortized Bluestein per-row (2x 2048 radix-2 FFTs) is well-optimized and the general radix-5 combine has overhead. Add hardcoded radix-3 and radix-5 butterflies (fewer mults, structured twiddles) to the combine. Single-transform mixed-radix is already ~2x (Bluestein plan-build elim); this targets the batched case. Control bench rfft_batch_64x1003_bluestein added.

title: fj-ad ReduceSum VJP + broadcast_g_to_shape_with_axes widen scalar g to F64
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:1537-1561 ReduceSum VJP shortcuts scalar g via as_f64_scalar() and rebuilds the broadcast result as DType::F64. Same widening at crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:4728-4740 in broadcast_g_to_shape_with_axes. For F32/BF16/F16 g, the broadcast result is silently widened to F64; for Complex64/Complex128 g, as_f64_scalar returns None so the path is bypassed (correct). Acceptance: (a) detect g's dtype via dtype_for_literal when g is a scalar Value::Scalar(lit); (b) build the broadcast tensor with that dtype, using the matching literal kind (F32→from_f32, BF16→from_bf16_f32, etc.); (c) update both ReduceSum VJP and broadcast_g_to_shape_with_axes; (d) regression test: ReduceSum VJP with F32 input + F32 g produces an F32-typed gradient; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: ReduceSum VJP + broadcast_g_to_shape_with_axes scalar shortcuts now preserve g's dtype via dtype_for_literal + literal kind replication, instead of unconditionally widening to F64. title: gamma functions (lgamma/digamma) missing Complex64/Complex128 support

Lgamma and Digamma primitives reject complex dtypes with error:
"lgamma/digamma is not supported for complex dtypes"

JAX supports complex gamma functions, important for:
- Statistical mechanics (partition functions)
- Quantum field theory
- Complex-valued special function computations

## Evidence
- commit da679ab: added 5 ignored tests documenting the gap
- Tests: gamma_funcs_oracle.rs oracle_*_complex64_*, oracle_*_complex128_*

## Fix Required
Implement complex lgamma using Stirling's approximation or reflection formula.
Implement complex digamma as derivative of lgamma.

title: [perf][no-gaps][alien] fj-lax: SAFE persistent thread-pool to kill per-call OS-thread spawn
description:
THE systemic lever behind the work-scaled-threads band-aid (s9q4d): every threaded path uses std::thread::scope which spawns N OS threads PER CALL, sequentially (~3ms for 64). That's why flat-64 was 9x too slow at moderate n (reduction), why 1M exp was only 1.24x, and why mj9ip SIMD-exp underwhelmed. work_scaled_threads caps the damage by using FEWER threads at moderate n, but that LEAVES PARALLELISM ON THE TABLE (8 threads not 64). A persistent pool eliminates per-call spawn -> full 64-core parallelism at moderate n with ZERO spawn cost.

ROI (compute-bound moderate-n): exp_512k — flat-64 (spawn) ~4ms; work-scaled-8 1.37ms; PERSISTENT-64 ~= 512k/64*5ns (40us compute) + concat (4MB copy ~0.4ms) ~= 0.45ms. So ~3x over work-scaling, ~9x over flat. Biggest payoff: the MANY-SMALL-GEMM storm in blocked Cholesky/LU/QR trailing updates (thousands of per-call spawns).

SAFE design (no unsafe, no deps — the borrow wall is the reason rayon uses unsafe; sidestep it with OWNED chunks, not borrowed &mut):
- static POOL: OnceLock<Pool> with N worker threads spawned ONCE; workers loop recv on an mpsc work channel, run job, send result.
- Jobs are Box<dyn FnOnce()->Vec<f64> + Send + 'static>. For elementwise: capture (a) the input as Arc<Vec<f64>> CLONED (cheap — fj-core LiteralBufferStorage::F64 is already Arc<Vec<f64>>, line 984; ADD a pub accessor `arc_f64() -> Option<Arc<Vec<f64>>>` since only as_f64_slice exists), (b) a chunk range, (c) the op (fn ptr / 'static Copy closure). Worker computes that chunk into an OWNED Vec<f64> and returns it. All 'static -> SAFE.
- Main submits T jobs, collects T owned Vecs, CONCATs into the output Vec.
- Cost: the concat is one extra O(n) copy. Worth it for compute-bound (compute>>copy); for memory-bound ops it's NOT (copy ~= work) -> keep those on the existing direct-write scoped path. So: route COMPUTE-bound ops (transcendentals, expensive binary, FFT rows, GEMM row-blocks) through the pool; leave cheap memory-bound ops as-is.

GEMM variant: row-blocks are concatenable (each block is contiguous output rows) -> same owned-chunk + concat model fits, killing the per-call spawn across the Cholesky/LU trailing-update storm.

Proof obligations: bit-identical (each element/row computed by one worker, same order; concat preserves order). Test: pool result == serial == scoped-thread result, across thread counts. Bench: exp_512k, pow_1m, and a blocked-Cholesky-1024 (many small GEMMs) before/after. Target >=2x over the work-scaled baseline. Watch: deadlock (bound the channel / use rendezvous), panic propagation (catch_unwind in worker -> propagate), pool sizing (num_cpus), and the result-ordering on collect.
notes:
BUILT + MEASURED + REJECTED. Implemented a safe persistent pool (thread_pool.rs: round-robin per-worker MPSC channels, no shared recv-Mutex; OnceLock global; owned-chunk + concat). Bit-identical (parallel_fill_matches_serial_across_thread_counts 1-64 threads). Wired eval_exp through it (full 64-core, no spawn).
RESULT: REGRESSES vs the work-scaling already shipped. Pool exp_512k 2.12ms vs work-scaled 1.37ms; exp_4m 23.5ms vs ~10ms (~1.5-2.3x SLOWER).
ROOT CAUSE (definitive): in safe-Rust-no-unsafe you CANNOT have persistent workers write disjoint &mut slices of a caller-local output (that's the borrow wall rayon uses unsafe to cross). So the pool MUST use owned per-chunk Vecs + a final CONCAT copy. That concat (32MB at 4M) + the 64 per-chunk Vec allocs cost MORE than the per-call std::thread::scope spawn it eliminates. The spawn is ~3ms (64 threads) but work_scaled_threads already cuts that to ~8 threads (~0.4ms), while the concat is O(n) and unavoidable.
CONCLUSION: work_scaled_threads (scoped threads + direct-write + fewer-threads-to-limit-spawn) is the SAFE OPTIMUM for fj's parallelization lever. A persistent pool only wins if concat << spawn-saved, which never holds for sizeable elementwise/reduction outputs; the many-small-GEMM case also doesn't win (small concat ≈ small spawn, and matmul_thread_count already work-scales). thread_pool.rs left UNTRACKED (not deleted per RULE 1) in case a future unsafe-permitted or scoped-pool design revisits it. CLOSING — parallelization lever is at its safe optimum.
close_reason: Built+measured: persistent pool REGRESSES (concat copy + chunk allocs > spawn saved). Safe-Rust can't avoid the concat (borrow wall). work_scaled_threads is the safe optimum.

title: Concatenate overflows output axis for zero-element huge dims
description:
[HIGH] Latest Concatenate oracle coverage added normal and empty-array cases, but crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs still computes the concatenated axis with unchecked u32 addition at line 849: tensors.iter().map(|t| t.shape.dims[axis]).sum(). Valid zero-element tensors can carry very large dimensions because Shape::element_count multiplies in u64 and any zero dimension yields element_count=0, so TensorValue::new accepts shapes like [u32::MAX, 0] with an empty element vector.

Repro: create a = TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![u32::MAX, 0] }, vec![]) and b = TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![1, 0] }, vec![]), wrap them as Value::Tensor, set params { "dimension": "0" }, then call eval_primitive(Primitive::Concatenate, &[a, b], &params). Current debug/test builds panic when summing u32::MAX + 1; optimized builds can wrap the output axis to 0 and return the wrong shape.

Expected: use checked addition for the concatenated axis and return a typed EvalError when the output axis exceeds u32::MAX; also use checked multiplication for total output element count before allocating. Add a regression test using the zero-element huge-dim repro so it does not allocate.
close_reason: Rejected overflowing Concatenate output axes and checked output element products

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: threaded dense polygamma(n, x) — 20.5x

title: Pin CopySign signed-zero bit oracle coverage

title: fj-ad betainc FORWARD-mode JVP silently ignored a/b tangents where JAX raises (VJP counterpart of 7w6qi)
description: Completing the betainc grad-parity fix (reverse-mode VJP was 7w6qi). The forward-mode JVP (jvp_rule) computed the betainc output tangent as deriv*tangents[2] (the x-contribution) and IGNORED tangents[0]/tangents[1] (da/db). So differentiating betainc w.r.t. a or b in forward mode silently returned the x-only tangent (0 when dx=0) instead of the true a/b contribution -- JAX's defjvp registers betainc_grad_not_implemented for a and b, which RAISES. Unlike the monolithic reverse VJP (which had to NaN-poison because it cannot tell which inputs are differentiated), forward mode SEES the tangents, so it now matches JAX EXACTLY: raise (AdError::UnsupportedPrimitive) iff !is_zero_value(da) or !is_zero_value(db); else the supported x-tangent (exact). Test betainc_jvp_raises_on_a_b_differentiation (x-tangent dx=1.728 ok; a-tangent and b-tangent raise). Full jvp suite 75/75.

title: ReduceWindow trace panics on zero stride
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs still has a loose ReduceWindow shape-inference path that parses window_dimensions/window_strides with filter_map and never rejects zero. Runtime eval_reduce_window was hardened in fj-lax, but trace inference was not. Repro: create a TraceContext with one rank-1 F64 input shaped [3], call process_primitive(Primitive::ReduceWindow, &[input], params) with window_dimensions="2", window_strides="0", padding="same". Expected: Err(TraceError::ShapeInferenceFailed or InvalidPrimitiveParam) matching fj-lax runtime rejection. Actual: line 1761 reaches input_dim.div_ceil(stride) with stride == 0 and panics in debug/test builds. The same parser also silently drops malformed stride/window tokens instead of rejecting them, so fix should use strict parsing, require lengths equal rank, require positive windows/strides, and add trace regression coverage for zero, malformed, and short params.
close_reason: Fixed: strict parsing with validation for window_dimensions/window_strides length==rank and positive values. Added 5 regression tests.

title: fj-lax SVD: small singular values only accurate to sqrt(eps) due to AtA normal-equations
description: eval_svd_real (crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs:1282) forms ata = A^T A (n x n) and runs jacobi_eigendecomposition_cyclic on it, then sigma = sqrt(eigenvalue). Forming A^T A squares the condition number, so small singular values are accurate only to ~sqrt(eps)*||A|| instead of LAPACK/JAX's eps*||A|| (Golub-Kahan or one-sided Jacobi). Repro: rank-2 3x3 [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[5,7,9]] gives smallest sigma=2.69e-8 instead of ~1e-14 (test oracle_svd_rank_deficient_3x3_reconstructs in linalg_oracle.rs). Reconstruction is unaffected. FIX (tractable, no C deps): one-sided Jacobi SVD applies rotations directly to A's columns to orthogonalize them, avoiding the A^T A squaring -> full small-sigma accuracy. Not much more complex than current AtA path. eval_svd (complex, line 1180) has the same aha = A^H A pattern.

title: cumsum/cumprod missing Complex64/Complex128 support
description:
## Summary
Cumsum and Cumprod primitives reject complex dtypes (Complex64, Complex128) with error:
"expected numeric tensor"

JAX supports complex cumulative operations:
```python
import jax.numpy as jnp
x = jnp.array([1+1j, 2+2j, 3+3j], dtype=jnp.complex64)
jnp.cumsum(x)  # works: [1+1j, 3+3j, 6+6j]
```

## Evidence
- commit f26a4d9: added 11 ignored tests documenting the gap
- Tests: cumulative_oracle.rs oracle_cumsum_complex64_* and oracle_cumprod_complex64_*

## Fix Required
Update `is_numeric_tensor()` check in cumulative primitive evaluation to include Complex64/Complex128, then implement complex cumulative operations (component-wise addition for cumsum, proper complex multiplication for cumprod).
close_reason: Fixed in commit 7b75b3d - added complex cumsum/cumprod support

JAX/NumPy observable behavior is NaN (inf - inf and nan - nan), but the safe optimizer can rewrite to literal 0. Similarly x * 0 at x=+Inf should remain NaN but can fold to 0. Move these boundary-unsafe rewrites behind numerically_unsafe_rules and add conformance tests for NaN/Inf preservation.

title: Harden cache golden verifier against truncated snapshots
description: The fj-cache golden stability harness in crates/fj-cache/src/stability.rs verifies expected cache-key snapshots by zipping the supplied golden vector with the current generated vector. That silently ignores length mismatches, so a truncated golden snapshot can pass without checking newly added reference cases. It also ignores description mismatches, so reordered or renamed golden entries can produce confusing drift reports. Fix verify_golden_keys to report reference-count mismatches and per-index description mismatches before digest comparison, then add regression tests showing truncated and renamed golden snapshots fail. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-cache stability, rch cargo check -p fj-cache --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-cache --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Hardened fj-cache golden verifier to reject truncated, renamed, and extra cache-key snapshot vectors; added regression coverage; validated with remote rch cache stability tests, remote rch check/clippy, fmt, and UBS.

title: Add Complex64 scalar Exp VJP numerical verification
description: Exp is a fundamental transcendental primitive. For complex z = a+bi, exp(z) = e^a (cos(b) + i sin(b)). VJP: d/dz exp(z) = exp(z) → g_z = g * exp(z). Add exp_vjp_numerical_complex64 with concrete inputs to pin both Complex64 dtype preservation and correct Euler-formula complex exp. Acceptance: (a) test asserts Complex64 dtype; (b) values match closed-form within f32 tolerance; (c) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Added exp_vjp_numerical_complex64: z=0+0.5i, g=1+0i → g_z = exp(z) = cos(0.5) + i sin(0.5) ≈ 0.8776 + 0.4794i via Euler formula. Asserts Complex64 dtype + values within f32 tolerance. ad_numerical_verification 69 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 5f65e83.

title: fj-ad: sort VJP averaged tied gradients, breaking JVP/VJP transpose duality + JAX parity
description: Sort VJP averaged gradients across tied-value groups (symmetric subgradient). But JAX scatters by the inverse argsort permutation (no averaging) — the exact transpose of the sort JVP (which gathers the tangent by argsort). After the sort JVP was fixed to gather by the stable permutation (bgyxy), the averaging VJP was no longer its transpose: jvp(sort) and vjp(sort) disagreed at ties. x=[2,1,1], g=[3,9,6]: averaging gave [6,6,6], JAX/transpose gives [6,3,9]. Fixed VJP to scatter by the stable inverse permutation; added <Ju,v>==<u,Jtv> transpose-duality test.
close_reason: Fixed: sort VJP scatters tied gradients by the stable inverse argsort permutation (no averaging), matching JAX and making it the exact transpose of the sort JVP. Tests sort_vjp_routes_tied_gradients_by_stable_permutation ([6,3,9]) + sort_jvp_vjp_are_transposes_at_ties pass. fj-ad 343/0, conformance jvp 76/0 ad 93/0.
