title: [parity-gap] safe egraph reciprocal-as-div and neg-zero erase output dtype
description:
Review finding:
- safe_algebraic_rules contains reciprocal-as-div: (reciprocal ?a) => (div 1 ?a)
- safe_algebraic_rules contains neg-zero: (neg 0) => 0
- Both introduce untyped literal constants (1 and 0) that erase the output dtype
- E.g., reciprocal(x_f32) should return f32, but (div 1 x_f32) uses untyped literal 1
- E.g., neg(0.0_f32) should return f32, but the rewrite returns untyped 0

Fix: Move both rules to numerically_unsafe_rules.
close_reason: Moved neg-zero and reciprocal-as-div to numerically_unsafe_rules; both erase output dtype via untyped literals

title: [parity-gap] Primitive max/min NaN semantics use fmax/fmin behavior
description: Review of the recent safe-egraph max/min absorption changes exposed an underlying parity gap in fj-lax and its oracle tests. crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs evaluates Primitive::Max and Primitive::Min with f64::max/f64::min, and crates/fj-conformance/tests/min_max_oracle.rs asserts max(NaN, 5)=5 and min(NaN, 5)=5 while calling that Rust/JAX behavior. Official JAX docs for jax.numpy.maximum and jax.numpy.minimum say one NaN operand produces NaN; JAX documents fmax/fmin as the NaN-ignoring variants. Repro: eval_primitive(Primitive::Max, &[Value::scalar_f64(f64::NAN), Value::scalar_f64(5.0)], &BTreeMap::new()) returns 5.0; eval_primitive(Primitive::Min, same inputs) returns 5.0. Expected for JAX maximum/minimum/lax max-min parity is NaN propagation unless this primitive is intentionally re-scoped as fmax/fmin, in which case ProgramSpec/tests/docs/egraph rules must say so. Fix the primitive contract, elementwise and reduction oracle tests, and egraph max/min safety coverage against real JAX semantics.
close_reason: Fixed Primitive::Max/Min and ReduceMax/ReduceMin float NaN semantics to propagate NaN like JAX maximum/minimum; added scalar, tensor, and reduction coverage; validated with rch conformance/fj-lax tests, fmt, workspace check, clippy, and UBS.

title: [modes-of-reasoning] functional-purity-vs-python-compat
description:
FrankenJAX quarantines impurity at boundaries but cannot enforce it. Callback flavors (Pure/Io) declare intent without verification. Effect system is tracking-only and does not inform optimization. FFI boundary trusts C functions without runtime contracts.

Key locations:
- fj-ffi/src/callback.rs:3-19 (unverified Pure/Io distinction)
- fj-core/src/lib.rs:974,990 (effect tracking as metadata only)
- fj-dispatch/src/lib.rs:30 (V1 scope: tracking only comment)
- fj-ffi/src/call.rs:26-34 (trust-based FFI safety contract)

Suggested work:
1. Add runtime callback purity inference (detect if pure callback mutates shared state)
2. Promote effect tokens from observational to participatory in dispatch ordering
3. Add FFI contract assertions (pre/post-condition hooks for C boundary)
close_reason: Added PurityConfig with verify_idempotency flag in 0bb2491. Strict mode double-invokes Pure callbacks to detect non-determinism; impure callbacks that return different results trigger PurityViolation error. Addresses the gap between declared and actual purity.

title: [code-review] Egraph bitwise_xor self rewrite erases unsigned dtype
description:
Review finding from commit 1ccef8f in `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs`.

Defect: safe egraph rule `bitwise-xor-self` rewrites `(bitwise_xor ?a ?a)` to the bare egraph literal `0`. `egraph_to_jaxpr()` lowers bare numeric literals as `Literal::I64`, so optimizing an unsigned bitwise XOR self-cancel changes the output dtype/value representation from `U32(0)`/`U64(0)` to `I64(0)`. The type-blind egraph cannot safely introduce an untyped integer literal for typed bitwise operations.

Concrete repro:
1. Build a Jaxpr with one equation: `out = BitwiseXor(v1, v1)`.
2. Evaluate the original with `Value::scalar_u64(9_007_199_254_740_993)`: expected/output is `Value::scalar_u64(0)`.
3. Optimize with `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` and evaluate with the same input.
4. Actual optimized program returns an I64 zero (or otherwise violates unsigned dtype preservation) because the rewrite emits bare `Num(0)`.
5. Add/run a conformance regression with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics bitwise_xor_self_preserves_unsigned_dtype -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: remove `bitwise-xor-self` from the type-blind egraph rules, or add typed-zero lowering/proofs so U32/U64 inputs produce same-dtype zero. Cover scalar and tensor U32/U64 cases.
close_reason: Duplicate/subset of frankenjax-hf2l, which tracks the broader egraph literal dtype erasure issue including bitwise_xor unsigned zeros.

title: fj-ad: sort JVP independently sorts the tangent instead of permuting by primal order
description: Sort JVP was ep_p(Sort, [tangent]) — it sorted the tangent by its OWN values. But JAX _sort_jvp gathers the tangent by argsort(primal): the tangent must follow the SAME permutation that sorts the primal. e.g. x=[3,1,2] sorts via perm [1,2,0]; tangent [0.1,0.2,0.3] must become [0.2,0.3,0.1], not sort()=[0.1,0.2,0.3]. So forward-mode AD through sort was wrong for any tangent not already in primal order. Fixed with sort_jvp_value (per-axis-slice stable argsort of primal, gather tangent by it). VJP was already correct (unsort via argsort).
close_reason: Fixed: sort JVP now permutes the tangent by the primal's argsort (sort_jvp_value), matching JAX _sort_jvp. Test test_sort_jvp_permutes_tangent_by_primal_order_not_sorts_it ([3,1,2],[0.1,0.2,0.3]->[0.2,0.3,0.1]) passes. fj-ad 342/0, conformance jvp 76/0.

title: BroadcastInDim silently wraps oversized shape dims
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs eval_broadcast_in_dim parses the required shape param as i64, accepts any non-negative value, then casts with d as u32 at line 612. A shape dimension above u32::MAX silently wraps instead of returning an error. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::BroadcastInDim, &[Value::scalar_i64(1)], &params) with params shape=4294967296. The implementation converts that dim to 0, computes total=0, and can return an empty tensor with shape [0] instead of rejecting the impossible requested output shape. Larger multi-dimensional shapes can also overflow the product at line 625/702 or attempt enormous allocation. Expected behavior: reject negative and out-of-u32-range dimensions before casting, and use checked multiplication for output element counts. Add regression tests covering shape=4294967296 and a product overflow case.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-range BroadcastInDim shape dims and checked output element products

title: fj-lax QR: WY-blocked Householder QR using matmul_2d trailing updates (18 GF/s -> 2-4x)
description: Profile: linalg/qr_1024x1024_f64 = 79.8ms (~18 GF/s) at 995430b6 — naive per-reflector Householder trailing update is memory-bound. matmul_2d hits 100+ GF/s. Lever: blocked Householder QR (LAPACK dgeqrf/dorgqr): factor b-column panels, build compact WY (V n×b, T b×b), apply to the trailing matrix as GEMMs via matmul_2d (C -= V (Tᵀ (Vᵀ C))). Gate for large n (keep the bit-identical scalar/tiled path for small n so qr_tiled_reflector_bit_identical_to_scalar_column_loop still holds); blocked path verified by A=QR reconstruction tolerance (QR JAX-parity is tolerance 1e-12, not bit-exact). Target >=2x on qr_1024. Same-worker rch baseline + rebench; golden sha256 for small-n path unchanged.
notes:
PANEL-BLOCKING CONCLUSIVELY REJECTED (benched both sizes, source-restored):
  baseline (naive) qr_1024 = 79.8ms; blocked b=32 = 461ms; blocked b=128 = 349ms.
Larger blocks help (461->349) but never approach baseline — matmul_2d's per-call
packing + the panel-update structure can't beat the naive memory-bound 18 GF/s. The
naive per-reflector kernel is already near the memory-bandwidth limit and panel-blocking
adds GEMM/packing overhead without reducing total work enough.

ONLY remaining path = RECURSIVE E-G QR (large GEMMs from recursive halving + recursive T
assembly) — a major multi-session implementation. Deprioritize unless that is scheduled;
the attempt-1 WY machinery (qr_compact_wy_t/qr_block_apply, R=Tᵀ Q=T) is verified-correct
and reusable for it. Do NOT keep tuning block size (rejected-lever signal).

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax Cholesky1024: blocked full-Schur update primitive

title: fj-lax erfc mid-range [3.5,5): asymptotic floored at sqrt-eps relative; replace with Laplace continued fraction
description: erfc_approx evaluated the tail via the asymptotic series erfc(t) ~ exp(-t^2)/(t sqrt-pi) sum (-1)^k (2k-1)!!/(2t^2)^k, which has a relative-error FLOOR set by its smallest term: ~3.5e-6 at x=3.5, improving to ~1e-10 by x=5. So erfc(3.5/4/4.5) were only 6-9 significant figures vs XLA's ~15. Replaced with the constant-free Laplace continued fraction (DLMF 7.9.3) sqrt-pi e^{x^2} erfc(x) = 1/(x + (1/2)/(x + 1/(x + (3/2)/(x + ...)))) evaluated by modified Lentz -- accurate ~1e-13 relative across the whole tail [3.5, inf). Added an explicit infinity guard (erfc(+inf)=0, erfc(-inf)=2; the CF would give inf*0=NaN). Verified erfc(3.5..6) match scipy to rel under 1e-12 (were up to 3.5e-6 off); metamorphic erfc==1-erf preserved (x in [-3,3], under-3.5 branch unchanged); erfc_oracle 28, erf_oracle 30 pass.

title: Implement complex rem and atan2 in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex remainder and atan2 to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: Same match arm as complex pow — the catch-all at line 160.
HOW: Complex rem: z1 % z2 = z1 - z2 * floor(z1/z2) using complex division and floor on real/imag parts separately. Complex atan2: generally not well-defined for complex numbers — JAX raises an error. We should match JAX behavior: return a principled UnsupportedOperation error (not "not yet implemented").
WHY: Completing the binary op coverage. For atan2, the right answer is an explicit "not supported for complex" error rather than "not yet implemented" (which implies deferred work).
DEPENDS ON: Complex pow implementation (shares the same code path).
TESTING: Test complex rem for simple cases. Test that complex atan2 returns UnsupportedOperation.
ACCEPTANCE: No "not yet implemented" string remains in apply_complex_binary.
close_reason: Superseded: restructuring complex arithmetic beads. The dependency on frankenjax-fij was wrong (pow already implemented), and the scope needs expansion.

title: [code-review] egraph Rev cancellation is order-sensitive on axes
description:
**File:** crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:471
**Severity:** MEDIUM (missed optimization, not correctness)

The `cancels_shape_chain` function uses order-sensitive comparison for Rev axes:

```rust
(Primitive::Rev, Primitive::Rev) => rev_axes(&previous.params) == rev_axes(&current.params),
```

When axes are specified in different orders (e.g., `axes="0,1"` vs `axes="1,0"`), the comparison fails even though the operations are identical and should cancel.

**Fix:** Sort the axes before comparison or use a BTreeSet.
close_reason: Fixed Rev cancellation by canonicalizing axes and adding regression coverage

title: [perf][no-gaps][alien-artifact] fj-lax: Strassen / recursive GEMM (O(n^2.807)) for large Dot
description:
The parallelization MODEL is exhausted at its safe optimum (work_scaled_threads; persistent-pool 8phkx measured-rejected). Next genuinely-different lever = a different COMPLEXITY CLASS for GEMM: Strassen (7 mults instead of 8 per 2x2 block recursion -> O(n^2.807)).

VIABILITY CONFIRMED: Dot parity vs JAX is TOLERANCE-based — dot_oracle/dot_general_oracle use assert_close at 1e-10..1e-14, NOT bit-exact (no to_bits vs JAX). XLA's own GEMM isn't bit-identical to naive ijk either. So Strassen is allowed IF it stays within ~1e-10. NOTE: the INTERNAL matmul_2d_ikj_bit_identical tests are fj's own invariant on the EXISTING kernel — add Strassen as a SEPARATE recursive path (gated for large n, base-case cutoff -> existing matmul_2d), leaving those tests valid.

DESIGN: recursive bisection M=N=K (pad to even / handle odd by peeling a border row/col-block with the base kernel). 7 products of (n/2)^3 + 18 add/sub of (n/2)^2. Base-case cutoff ~256-512 (below that, adds dominate -> call matmul_2d). Temp buffers reused per level (arena). 

ROI / RISK (be honest): 1-level ~1.1x, 2-level ~1.28x, 3-level ~1.47x at 1024^3; deeper -> more but add/sub + temp-alloc overhead grows AND numerical error grows (each level adds subtractions -> cancellation). Practical ~1.3-1.8x at 2048^3-4096^3; reaching >=2.0 needs deep recursion which risks exceeding 1e-10 for ill-conditioned inputs. CALIBRATE: measure error vs naive on random + ill-conditioned matrices at each depth; cap depth where rel-err < 1e-11 (margin under the 1e-10 oracle). Bench 2048^3 / 4096^3 same-binary A/B.
ALTERNATIVE if Strassen underwhelms: proper BLIS cache-blocking (pack A into L2-resident MC×KC, B into L3-resident KC×NC, reuse across M) — communication-avoiding, bit-identical (same accumulation order). The earlier column-pack attempt failed because B was L3-resident; a full 5-loop BLIS with A-packing is different. Target the 1024^3 L3-bandwidth wall (currently 5.5x on 64 cores).
notes:
SHIPPED complex eigh/SVD O(n⁴)→O(n³) (e1a90069, on the now-correct kernel ac0f26bd).
Routed complex Hermitian eigh + complex thin-SVD from the max-pivot Jacobi (O(n²)
off-diagonal scan per rotation) to row-cyclic sweeps. Same-binary A/B (complex_jacobi
_cyclic_ab_timing): n=48 3.28x, n=96 3.31x, n=160 6.83x — clean O(n³) scaling.
Isomorphism: complex_jacobi_cyclic_matches_maxpivot (identical sorted spectrum vs
retained reference + VᴴHV diagonal, n≤33); 1146 fj-lax lib + 68 conformance pass.
Mirrors the real-path cyclic win (2.18x ec305146). GEMM microkernel-intensity lever
(MR=6/NR=8 / f64x4 SIMD) for the L3-resident regime remains the next swing there.
close_reason: Completed/routed: B-panel packing and KC-blocked GEMM are already live; pass138 tested the remaining NR8 scalar-tile lever and rejected it on same-worker RCH (NR4 22.040ms vs NR8 23.963ms, 0.920x). Follow-up frankenjax-vgd3x claimed for Strassen or safe-SIMD primitive.

Same-worker RCH ts1 rejected the lever: NR4 control 22.040ms [20.866,23.286] vs NR8 candidate 23.963ms [21.521,28.008], speedup 0.920x midpoint. Source restored; tensor_contraction.rs diff empty. title: Resolve complex select condition and clamp stubs in fj-lax arithmetic.rs (7 stubs)
description:
CONTEXT: 7 of the 14 stubs in fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs are in Select and Clamp: 4x select with complex condition (lines ~1110, ~1162, ~1240, ~1315) say complex condition not yet implemented for select. 3x clamp with complex value/lo/hi (lines ~1401, ~1414, ~1426) say complex arithmetic not yet implemented.

ANALYSIS: Neither needs real implementation. JAX does NOT support complex conditions for select (must be bool) or complex clamp (no total order). These are correct errors with wrong wording that implies deferred work.

CHANGES: 1) Replace all 4 select messages with: select condition must be boolean, got complex dtype. 2) Replace all 3 clamp messages with: clamp is not supported for complex dtypes. 3) Ensure none contain not yet.

TESTING: Test select(complex_cond, a, b) returns error mentioning boolean. Test clamp(complex_value, lo, hi) returns error mentioning complex dtypes. Add targeted error-message tests. Verify all existing tests pass.

ACCEPTANCE: All 7 stubs replaced with permanent descriptive errors. Zero not yet implemented in select/clamp.

title: eval_det/eval_slogdet silently drop imaginary part for complex input (wrong result)
description: eval_det (linalg.rs ~2651) and eval_slogdet (~2682) do a.iter().map(|(re,_im)| re) and compute the determinant of the REAL PART only, returning a real scalar — JAX returns the COMPLEX determinant. Silent-wrong (worse than fail-closed). Fix: complex LU (product of pivots × (-1)^swaps) for the complex branch; det → complex scalar (input complex dtype); slogdet → (complex unit sign = det/|det|, real logabsdet = ln|det|), sign/logabsdet=(0,-inf) on singular. Verify vs closed-form (e.g. det(diag(1+i,1+i))=(1+i)^2=2i) + reconstruction.
close_reason: Fixed 1483e008: eval_det/eval_slogdet now compute the complex determinant via complex_det (LU, product of complex pivots × (-1)^swaps) instead of dropping the imaginary part. det→complex scalar; slogdet→complex unit sign + real logabsdet; singular→(0,-inf). Verified det(diag(1+i))=2i, det([[2+i,3-i],[0,1+2i]])=5i, slogdet sign=i logabsdet=ln2. 1151 lib + 56 conformance pass.

So Max,Min,ReduceMax,ReduceMin,Argmax,Argmin,Cummax,Cummin all support complex in JAX via (real,imag) lexicographic order. fj-lax rejects complex on these via extremum_along_axis/eval_binary_elementwise/eval_reduce_axes/eval_cumulative. All 6 oracle tests un-ignored + strengthened with imag-tiebreak golden values. fj-lax 988 pass, clippy clean. lt/le/gt/ge, rem, clamp correctly remain unsupported.

title: complex Hermitian Jacobi eigendecomposition returns wrong eigenvalues (not a similarity transform)
description:
complex_jacobi_eigendecomposition (crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs ~1453) — driving the
COMPLEX eigh path (eval_eigh ~2246) and complex thin-SVD via AᴴA (~1178) — does NOT
preserve the spectrum. 2×2 Hermitian repro:
  H = [[0.209, -0.0865+0.412i], [-0.0865-0.412i, 0.449]]
  true eigenvalues (exact 2×2): [0.7668, -0.1088]  (trace 0.658, det -0.0834)
  kernel returns:                [0.6668, -0.0088]  (trace 0.658 OK, product -0.0059 != det)
A unitary similarity preserves trace AND det; kernel preserves trace but not det, so its
per-pivot complex rotation is not a valid similarity. Vᴴ·H·V keeps a 0.278 off-diagonal;
the kernel force-zeros a[p][q], masking the under-rotation (returned 'eigenvalues' are the
diagonal of the force-zeroed but incompletely-rotated matrix). Iteration-order-sensitive: a
row-cyclic port vs the max-pivot kernel give DIFFERENT spectra at n=4 (-1.293 vs -1.322) —
impossible for a correct (order-invariant) Jacobi. Real symmetric path
(jacobi_eigendecomposition_cyclic) is CORRECT; only the COMPLEX rotation is suspect (likely
the atan half-angle or the Dᴴ·G·D unitary assembly, lines ~1493-1556). No complex-eigh
parity test exists (under-tested). FIX: correct the complex rotation so VᴴHV is diagonal;
THEN the O(n⁴)->O(n³) cyclic perf lever (cl9xa) ships on a correct kernel. Parity impact:
complex eigh/SVD eigenvalues may diverge from JAX today.
notes:
ATTEMPTED FIX INSUFFICIENT (do not just change the angle). Replacing the angle with
2θ=atan2(2|apq|, app−aqq) (correct quadrant when app<aqq) did NOT diagonalize:
Vᴴ H V off-diagonal stayed at EXACTLY |H[0][1]|=0.2784490233835116 (bit-identical
before/after), while diag(VᴴHV)=w held (1.4e-17). The unitary V preserves the
off-diagonal MAGNITUDE exactly yet changes the diagonal — so the defect is in the
phase realification / U assembly (lines ~1493-1556), not the Givens angle. Note the
in-code comment claims D=diag(1,e^{-iφ}) at q, but to realify A[p][q]=|apq|e^{iφ} you
need conj(D[q][q])=e^{-iφ} ⇒ D[q][q]=e^{+iφ}; the sign/placement of the phase looks
inverted, so the 'realified' pivot fed to the real Givens may not actually be real,
leaving |off| unchanged. NEXT: derive U so that UᴴHU zeros the (p,q) block for a
2×2 Hermitian and unit-test Vᴴ H V=diag BEFORE wiring; the force-zero of a[p][q]
masks any residual. Regression test complex_jacobi_diagonalizes_hermitian +
hermitian_test_matrix(n) were drafted (reverted with the fix attempt) — re-add them.
close_reason: Fixed in ac0f26bd: U was not D·G (preserved trace not det); corrected to U=D·G=[[c,-s],[s·e^{-iφ},c·e^{-iφ}]]. VᴴHV=diag(w) verified n≤33 (complex_jacobi_diagonalizes_hermitian); all 1145 fj-lax lib + 56 linalg conformance tests pass.

title: eval_solve fails closed on complex A/b — JAX supports complex jnp.linalg.solve
description:
eval_solve (crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs ~2381) returns Unsupported 'complex linear
solve is unsupported in V1' for any Complex64/Complex128 A or b, but jnp.linalg.solve
supports complex — a no-gaps behavior-parity gap (fail-closed feature gap, not wrong
results). Fill it with a complex Gaussian-elimination solve (partial pivoting on the
augmented [A|B]), mirroring the real path's contract (error on singular, vector and
matrix b, dtype promotion). Reuse the complex helpers added for eig. Verify A·x=b
reconstruction for random non-singular complex systems + dtype promotion
(C64+C64→C64, anything-C128 or real-F64-mixed → C128). Update eval_solve_rejects_
complex_input test to assert correct solve.
close_reason: Implemented 456c92c9: complex jnp.linalg.solve via complex_solve_system (Gaussian elim, partial pivoting, augmented [A|B], one factorization for all RHS). Vector/matrix b, singular→inf/NaN (matches real path), numpy/JAX dtype promotion. Verified: closed-form diag(1+i) + general 3x3/3x2 A·X=B residual <1e-10; 1151 lib + 56 conformance pass.

title: fj-lax: complex sort/argsort/topk parity (JAX lexicographic real,imag)
description: VERIFIED false parity gap. fj-lax sort_key() rejects Complex64/128, so Sort/Argsort/TopK error on complex. But JAX lax.sort DOES support complex: _operands_to_keys (jax/_src/lax/lax.py) splits complex into [real, imag] float keys and sorts lexicographically. NumPy confirms: np.sort([3+1j,1+2j,1+1j,2+0j]) = [1+1j,1+2j,2+0j,3+1j]. Fix: add SortKey::Complex(f64,f64) variant ordered by (real total_cmp, then imag total_cmp). Un-ignore oracle_sort/argsort/topk_complex64 tests in fj-conformance (they already .expect() success). Add non-zero-imag golden test vs numpy.
close_reason: Implemented SortKey::Complex(f64,f64) lexicographic (real,imag) ordering in fj-lax sort_key/compare_sort_keys; Sort/Argsort/TopK now support Complex64/128. Un-ignored + strengthened 3 conformance oracle tests with non-zero-imag golden values vs numpy. fj-lax 988 pass, clippy clean.

title: [code-review] safe egraph neg-neg skips bool validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `neg-neg` in `safe_algebraic_rules` while default optimization is safety mode.
- `fj-lax` `eval_neg` rejects bool scalar/tensor inputs.
- The safe rewrite `neg(neg(x)) => x` can remove both validation operations and return an invalid input directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Build a Jaxpr with one input `x` and equations `v2 = Neg(x)`, `out = Neg(v2)`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::Scalar(Literal::Bool(true))]`. Expected/current original behavior: `eval_jaxpr` returns `EvalError::TypeMismatch` from `eval_neg` with detail `expected numeric scalar, got bool`.
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return `Value::Scalar(Literal::Bool(true))`.
4. Add regression coverage beside the existing `numerical_safety_mode_preserves_*` tests and run `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_neg_neg_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph neg_neg_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `neg-neg` out of safe mode, or require typed lowering evidence proving the inner neg validation already succeeded before eliminating both operations. Audit similar remaining safe unary rewrites such as `abs-neg`, trig/hyperbolic neg rules, `reciprocal-as-div`, and `pow-neg-one` for validation/dtype parity before declaring the family safe.
close_reason: Moved neg-neg to numerically_unsafe_rules; bypasses eval_neg bool validation

title: Build oracle parity recapture matrix and drift gate
description: Reality-check gap: README/FEATURE_PARITY report 848 JAX 0.9.2 fixture cases, but the V1 spec requires scoped compatibility to stay tied to the live legacy oracle rather than a one-time capture. Acceptance: enumerate every committed fixture bundle and its legacy test anchor; add a periodic strict recapture command path for transforms/RNG/linalg/FFT/dtype/composition; emit a machine-readable drift report with added/removed/changed cases; document the exact JAX/jaxlib/Python version; add a conformance test or artifact-schema test that fails if the recapture manifest omits a fixture family.
design: Revised plan-space design: make oracle evidence a maintained product surface, not a one-time fixture dump. Build a registry of fixture families, legacy anchors, capture commands, environment versions, expected artifact paths, drift classes, and replay commands. The work should improve future contributors by making it obvious when fixture evidence is stale, missing, or weaker than README/FEATURE_PARITY claims.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Inventory every committed oracle fixture family and link each to legacy test/module anchors, capture script, output artifact, parity runner, and current case count.
2. Add or update a machine-readable oracle recapture manifest covering transforms, RNG, linalg/FFT, dtype promotion, transform composition, higher-rank/manual oracle families, and any intentionally excluded scope.
3. Add a strict recapture/drift command path that records JAX, jaxlib, Python, platform, x64 flag, seed policy, and legacy source revision; no fallback-generated data may be labeled strict.
4. Emit a drift report with added, removed, changed, flaky, timeout, unsupported, and excluded-scope buckets plus replay commands for changed cases.
5. Unit tests: validate manifest parsing, family completeness, fixture-count reconciliation, version-field presence, and drift classification.
6. E2E script/logging: add or update a script/test path that runs a representative strict recapture dry run or fixture verification and writes a detailed JSON log with command, env, fixture family, counts, duration, and artifact refs.
7. CI/schema gate: fail if README/FEATURE_PARITY fixture claims exceed manifest-backed evidence or if a parity_green row lacks fixture/report coverage.
8. Update docs only from generated manifest/report data; preserve all existing fixture families and do not drop any prior coverage.
close_reason: Oracle recapture matrix and drift gate are implemented and validated; remaining live fixture refresh failures are tracked in frankenjax-cstq.21.

title: Bootstrap live vision-to-evidence map before implementation
description:
Reality-check gap: the final vision-to-evidence dashboard is intentionally downstream of the evidence lanes, but users and implementers need an early red/yellow control surface before those lanes complete. This bead bootstraps the map and checker skeleton without pretending parity is green.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define the initial machine-readable evidence-map schema for README claims, FEATURE_PARITY rows, V1 spec MUSTs, artifact families, tests, e2e scripts, replay commands, and bead links.
2. Seed the map from current docs and open beads with explicit red/yellow states for missing evidence; no green row is allowed unless the current artifact/test is named and replayable.

4. Add an e2e bootstrap script with detailed JSON logs: scanned docs, seeded claim rows, linked beads, missing evidence counts, red/yellow/green counts, artifact refs, and replay command.

title: Define shared E2E forensic logging contract
description:
Reality-check gap: the current child beads require e2e scripts, detailed JSON logs, and replay commands, but users need one shared forensic logging contract so evidence is comparable across oracle, transform, TTL, cache, performance, durability, docs, and integration lanes.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a shared JSON schema for e2e evidence logs covering bead id, scenario id, command, working directory, environment fingerprint, feature flags, fixture/oracle ids, transform stack, strict/hardened mode, inputs, expected result, actual result, tolerance policy, error taxonomy class, timings, allocation counters, artifact paths, artifact hashes, replay command, status, and human-readable failure summary.
2. Define script conventions for location, naming, stdout/stderr, exit codes, artifact directories, sidecar eligibility, and stable log filenames so local runs, CI runs, and dashboard ingestion all behave the same way.
3. Add unit tests for schema parsing, required-field rejection, malformed log rejection, replay command presence, artifact hash validation, redaction policy, status classification, and forward-compatible unknown-field handling.
4. Add at least one bootstrap e2e script and golden/sample log that demonstrates detailed logging, replayability, failure classification, and dashboard-ready artifact references without requiring mocks.
5. Update implementation guidance so child beads cannot claim e2e coverage complete with ad hoc logging; they must either emit this shared schema or record a temporary adapter with an explicit migration bead.
6. Preserve existing logs and artifacts; migrate or adapt them without deleting evidence.
7. Final dashboard coverage must consume this schema directly and flag missing, stale, or non-replayable logs as actionable failures.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove validator behavior for valid, missing-field, malformed, stale-hash, redacted, and unknown-field cases.
- E2E script emits JSON logs with enough detail to replay the exact scenario and diagnose failures without external context.
- A dashboard ingestion check rejects nonconforming logs and prints detailed reasons.
- README or contributor docs describe the contract in user-facing terms with no loss of existing feature scope.
close_reason: E2E forensic logging contract complete - schema, scripts, examples, 86 log files, and validation all in place

title: Prove security and adversarial fuzz gates
description:
Reality-check gap: the README and V1 spec promise security controls for cache confusion, transform-order vulnerabilities, malformed graph/shape signatures, silent artifact corruption, and continuous fuzzing, but the current ambition beads only cover this indirectly. Users need an explicit adversarial evidence gate for strict/hardened safety claims.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Build a threat-model matrix for cache keys, transform ordering, malformed IR/Jaxpr graphs, shape and dtype signatures, sub-Jaxprs/control flow, serialized fixtures/logs, FFI boundaries, durability sidecars, and evidence ledger recovery paths.
2. Define adversarial fixture and fuzz seed families for invalid arity, missing variables, wrong dtype, wrong shape, corrupt params, corrupt sub-Jaxprs, cyclic or unreachable graph structure, hostile cache metadata, corrupt sidecars, malformed e2e logs, extreme shapes, NaN/Inf payloads, and malformed JSON.
3. Add comprehensive unit tests for parser/validator rejection, no-panic behavior, strict fail-closed decisions, hardened recovery audit logs, deterministic seed handling, corpus regression loading, timeout classification, and error-taxonomy links.
4. Add e2e/fuzz scripts with detailed JSON logs: target, corpus path, seed, case count, fuzz duration or deterministic replay count, panic status, crash status, timeout status, expected error class, actual error class, minimized repro path, artifact hashes, and replay command.
5. Wire the result into CI/reliability gates and the final dashboard so missing, stale, non-replayable, or non-deterministic adversarial evidence blocks security-green status.
6. Findings from this lane must become br beads with precise scope; do not hide gaps inside broad cleanup and do not delete or discard existing fuzz corpus/artifacts.
7. Coordinate with frankenjax-cstq.8 for the final taxonomy, frankenjax-cstq.6 for cache rows, frankenjax-cstq.3 for transform-proof rows, and frankenjax-cstq.5 for artifact-corruption rows, but keep this bead runnable early as a security evidence bootstrap.

Acceptance evidence:
- Threat-model rows have explicit controls, tests, e2e/fuzz evidence, owner, and strict/hardened outcome.
- Unit tests prove malformed inputs return typed errors or audited hardened recoveries instead of panics.
- E2E/fuzz scripts emit shared-schema forensic logs with exact replay commands and minimized repro artifacts.
- Dashboard ingestion rejects security-green if adversarial evidence is missing or stale.
close_reason: Security threat model complete: 9 categories, all green evidence, 100% fuzz coverage

title: Prove fresh-clone onboarding and documented command gates
description:
Reality-check gap: README.md is a user-facing onboarding contract, not just project prose. It lists source-build, verification, E2E orchestration, reliability, durability, fuzzing, and optional JAX-oracle commands, but the current plan mainly proves API examples. Users need clean-checkout proof that documented commands are current, classified, and replayable.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Inventory all README and key-document commands for clone/build, nightly toolchain, cargo build/check/test/clippy/fmt, conformance capture, e2e orchestration, reliability gates, durability batch, fuzz build/run, feature flags, and optional oracle/Python setup.
2. Classify every command as mandatory local smoke, CI gate, long-running, optional oracle, environment-specific, or schematic/non-executable, with explicit skip rationale and user-facing expectations.
3. Add comprehensive unit tests for command inventory parsing, stale script path detection, missing command detection, duplicate command ids, environment allowlist/redaction, expected-exit classification, skip-rationale validation, and replay command generation.
4. Add a fresh-clone or clean-worktree e2e smoke script with detailed JSON logs: command id, command, cwd, rustc/cargo versions, feature flags, environment allowlist, exit status, duration, stdout/stderr summary, artifact refs, skip reason, and replay command.
5. Cover at least build/check/test smoke, README example smoke, e2e orchestration smoke, durability command smoke, fuzz build smoke, and optional oracle setup dry-run or documented opt-in path without requiring network during normal CI.
6. Generate user-facing docs/status from the inventory so README commands do not drift from real scripts; failed or skipped rows must be visible rather than silently omitted.
7. Preserve existing documentation and command intent. If a command is wrong, replace it only with an evidence-backed direct fix and leave an audit trail.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove the command inventory rejects stale paths, missing replay commands, unredacted secrets, duplicate ids, and unjustified skips.
- E2E smoke script emits shared-schema forensic logs and exact replay commands from a clean checkout or clean-worktree simulation.
- The final dashboard can ingest onboarding command status and fail docs-green when user-facing commands drift.
- frankenjax-cstq.9 remains focused on public API examples; this bead owns source-build and documented command experience.

title: Prove decision/evidence ledger calibration and auditability
description:
Reality-check gap: README.md and the V1 spec promise a decision/evidence ledger that explains consequential runtime choices with actions, alternatives, loss-matrix justification, evidence signals, posterior/confidence values, and durable audit artifacts. Current beads cover TTL proof and cache policy, but not the ledger calibration/drift-confidence evidence users need to trust “why did the system do X?” answers.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a machine-readable ledger coverage matrix for cache hit/recompute, strict rejection, hardened recovery, fallback denial/allowance, optimization selection, durability recovery, transform admission, unsupported-scope decisions, and runtime budget/deadline outcomes.
2. For each decision class, require captured action, alternatives considered, loss matrix, evidence signals, posterior or confidence values, mode, user-visible consequence, artifact links, and replay command.
3. Add calibration and drift-confidence artifacts for ledger decisions: expected-vs-observed outcome rows, confidence buckets, drift thresholds, stale-calibration classification, and generated user-facing summaries.
4. Add comprehensive unit tests for ledger schema validation, missing alternatives, invalid loss matrices, non-monotonic confidence buckets, stale calibration, duplicate decision ids, redaction policy, durability hash binding, and strict/hardened divergence allowlists.
5. Add e2e audit-trail scripts with detailed JSON logs: scenario id, decision class, inputs, selected action, alternatives, loss matrix, evidence signals, confidence/posterior values, expected outcome, actual outcome, calibration bucket, ledger artifact hash, dashboard row, and replay command.
6. Coordinate with cache, security, error taxonomy, performance, and durability beads without blocking their early evidence capture; gaps found here must become br follow-up beads rather than hidden notes.
7. Preserve existing fj-ledger APIs and artifacts unless a replacement is fully proven, documented, and linked from compatibility evidence.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove schema, calibration, drift, durability binding, redaction, and strict/hardened divergence behavior.
- E2E scripts produce shared-schema forensic logs and durable ledger artifacts for every decision class.
- Dashboard ingestion fails ledger-green when calibration, confidence, replay, or durability evidence is missing or stale.
- User-facing docs generated from the ledger explain decisions without requiring source-code archaeology.
close_reason: Hardened decision ledger calibration validation

title: Prove numerical stability and platform determinism gates
description:
Reality-check gap: README.md promises numerically stable AD/linalg behavior, platform-independent special-math approximations, exact bit-pattern serialization, deterministic RNG, and NaN/Inf behavior consistent with JAX/IEEE expectations. Existing fixture beads cover important oracle breadth, but users need a dedicated stability and determinism gate that ties tolerances, edge cases, and platform drift to evidence.

Plan-space requirements:
1. Define a numerical-stability matrix for special math approximations, linalg decompositions, AD VJP/JVP/Jacobian/Hessian, FFT scaling, dtype promotion, complex arithmetic, RNG determinism, literal bit round-trips, NaN/Inf handling, division-by-zero behavior, and finite-difference fallback policy.
2. For each row, record input family, dtype, shape, edge-condition class, reference source, tolerance policy, stability guard, expected error or value behavior, artifact refs, and replay command.
3. Add oracle, property, and metamorphic coverage for near-singular matrices, clustered eigenvalues, ill-conditioned triangular solves, special-math tails, cancellation-prone formulas, empty/singleton dimensions, complex branch-sensitive values, deterministic RNG seeds, and NaN payload/Inf propagation cases.
4. Add comprehensive unit tests for tolerance selection, finite-difference step policy, non-finite comparison, NaN/Inf classification, literal bit preservation, deterministic serialization, platform-stability metadata, regression-threshold enforcement, and missing-edge-case detection.
5. Add e2e stability scripts with detailed JSON logs: case id, primitive/family, dtype, shape, seed, platform fingerprint, reference source, tolerance, max error, ulp or relative error where applicable, non-finite classification, stability guard path, artifact hash, and replay command.
6. Keep this bead distinct from frankenjax-cstq.7: .7 expands higher-rank/complex/linalg/FFT oracle fixtures, while this bead owns numerical guardrails, tolerance governance, platform-determinism evidence, and non-finite/stability reporting across the whole stack.
7. Do not loosen tolerances or hide unstable rows to make reports green; any unresolved instability must become an explicit br follow-up bead with user-visible impact.

Acceptance evidence:
- Unit tests prove tolerance, non-finite, serialization, deterministic RNG, and regression-threshold behavior.
- E2E stability scripts emit shared-schema forensic logs with exact replay commands and platform fingerprints.
- Dashboard ingestion rejects numerical-stability green if tolerance evidence, platform metadata, non-finite classification, or replay artifacts are missing or stale.
- User-facing docs summarize stability limits and evidence without weakening existing parity claims.
close_reason: Numerical stability matrix: 11 families passing

title: fj-ad: implement cummax/cummin VJP and JVP (were zero stubs)
description: fj-ad returned zeros_like for both the VJP and JVP of Cummax/Cummin, silently killing gradient flow through cumulative max/min. JAX defines the JVP as jvp(associative_scan(max|min)): the output tangent at each position follows the input element that achieved the running extremum; the VJP is its transpose (scatter cotangent to that source). Implemented cum_extreme_sources (running-argmax/argmin source per position, honouring axis + reverse), wired VJP (scatter, dtype-preserving) and JVP (gather, dtype-exact). Proof: 8 unit tests incl. finite-difference vs real cummax/cummin and the <Ju,v>=<u,Jt v> adjoint identity. All 303 fj-ad tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean.
close_reason: Implemented in crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs: cum_extreme_sources + VJP scatter + JVP gather. 8 new tests (analytic, finite-difference, adjoint identity); 303 fj-ad tests pass; clippy clean.

title: Handle complex max/min with magnitude comparison (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex max and min to eval_binary_elementwise_complex() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:179-191.
WHERE: The match at line 183-191 only allows add/sub/mul/div through; max/min hit the catch-all error at line 186.
HOW: JAX uses lexicographic comparison on (real, imag) for complex max/min. Implement: compare real parts first, then imag parts for tiebreaking. Return the element that "wins." This matches numpy.maximum/minimum behavior for complex.
WHY: Without this, vmap or reduce operations on complex tensors that use max/min will fail. JAX handles this case.
DEPENDS ON: None (independent of pow/rem/atan2).
TESTING: Test complex max/min: max((1+2i), (3+0i)) = (3+0i); min((1+2i), (1+1i)) = (1+1i). Edge case: equal real parts resolved by imag.
ACCEPTANCE: No "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" string in eval_binary_elementwise_complex for max/min.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex max/min in fj-lax with lexicographic comparison and direct tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [bug][parity] fj-lax: reduce_window SUM skips OOB padding (XLA pads with init=0.0)
description: eval_reduce_window generic loop (lib.rs:3058 'if in_bounds') and the rank2 f64 sum fast path (lib.rs:2648/2711) SKIP out-of-bounds padding taps. XLA ReduceWindow pads the input with the reduction init_value (0.0 for sum/add) and includes those taps in the reduction. For sum-like ops this diverges for signed-zero inputs (a -0.0 accumulator stays -0.0 where XLA lands +0.0). All fj sum paths skip consistently (no internal RED test), so this needs a JAX oracle to confirm direction+order before fixing — XLA reduce_window reduction ORDER is also unspecified, so bit-exact parity may require care. max/min paths are fine (init -inf/+inf is a no-op under skip). NEEDS JAX ORACLE before implementing.
notes: FIXED e8d86f6a: all reduce_window sum paths (generic N-D loop, rank-2 f64 sum dense+literal, both 3x3-same border helpers) now include OOB padding taps as the reduction init (0.0 for sum) instead of skipping — XLA pads with init and reduces the full window. Generic loop accumulates init for OOB (correct for ALL ops: sum +0, max/min combine ∓∞ = no-op); f64 sum fast paths add 0.0 full-window row-major (bit-identical to generic + valid-on-padded). max/min unchanged (skip ∓∞ already correct). Provable WITHOUT JAX via metamorphic test (same == valid-on-zero-padded, 3 paths, signed-zero/inf). fj-lax --lib 1123 passed 0 failed. Completes the conv+pooling padding-parity audit (lyfik/qpq55/aqcrk/czaum).

title: Add Complex64 scalar Sqrt VJP numerical verification
description: d/dz sqrt(z) = 1/(2*sqrt(z)) → g_z = g/(2*sqrt(z)). For z=3+4i: sqrt(z) = 2+i, 1/(2*(2+i)) = (4-2i)/20 = 0.2-0.1i, so g_z = 0.2-0.1i with g=1+0i. Test asserts Complex64 dtype + 1e-5 f32 tolerance. Workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Filed as test-coverage but surfaced a real bug: Sqrt VJP used scalar_value(2.0) (F64) which widened Complex64 sqrt_x to Complex128. Added new scalar_constant_matching_dtype(x, like) helper (dtype-aware sibling of scalar_value) and threaded it through Sqrt VJP. Test sqrt_vjp_numerical_complex64 pins z=3+4i → g_z=0.2-0.1i with Complex64 dtype. fj-ad 283 + ad_numerical_verification 71 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed b767db8.

title: vmap(reduce) rejected negative axes (eval normalizes them)

Same bug class as 5jcw4 (vmap sort wrong-axis). Fix: parse axes as i64, normalize negative against per-element rank, then shift +1.
close_reason: Fixed: batch_reduce now parses axes as i64, normalizes negatives against per-element rank, then shifts +1. Repro test_batch_reduce_sum_handles_negative_axis (axes=-1) now passes; full batching suite 182/0.

title: fj-lax pinv/lstsq: route through accurate one-sided Jacobi SVD (drop AtA-Gram)
description: pinv used pinv_gram/gram_pseudoinverse (form AtA or AAt, eigendecompose, 1/lambda=1/sigma^2) -- squared condition number gave small singular values only sqrt(eps) relative accuracy, propagating into pinv and lstsq for ill-conditioned A. Replaced with SVD-based pinv_svd = V diag(1/sigma for sigma>rcond*sigma_max) U^T via shared one_sided_jacobi_svd_real core (extracted from eval_svd_real). JAX-exact definition. Proof pinv_ill_conditioned_2x2_svd_accurate: Sigma=diag(1,1e-6) rotated, SVD-pinv matches analytic to <1e-7 rel vs old Gram ~2e-4. lstsq inherits via pinv. Gram path deleted (pinv-only).

title: Make BufferView slicing reject overflowing ranges
description:
Skill: /multi-pass-bug-hunting

Verified issue: crates/fj-runtime/src/buffer.rs BufferView::slice currently computes offset + len before checking bounds. For inputs such as offset = usize::MAX and len = 1, debug builds panic on arithmetic overflow and optimized builds can wrap before reaching the slice range, violating the documented contract that out-of-bounds ranges return None.

Reproduction: call BufferView::from_buffer(&Buffer::new(vec![1, 2, 3], DeviceId(0))).slice(usize::MAX, 1). The current implementation panics instead of returning None.

Expected fix: use checked_add for the end offset, return None on overflow, and keep existing valid/boundary slice behavior unchanged. Add a unit test covering the overflow case.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 9ad7ba8: BufferView::slice now uses checked_add and returns None on overflow

title: [bug][parity] fj-lax: argmax/argmin diverge from JAX for negative-NaN (-NaN) inputs
description: JAX argmax/argmin reducer (_ArgMinMaxReducer) picks a candidate if comparator(v,acc) OR isnan(v) — ANY NaN (sign-agnostic) is selected, seeded with the identity (∓inf). fj uses total_cmp which ranks +NaN as max / -NaN as min, seeded with the first element. So +NaN (the common NaN) matches JAX, but a -NaN (sign bit set) is missed: argmax over a slice containing -NaN returns the finite max index instead of the -NaN index (JAX returns the -NaN index). Affects dense f64 path (tensor_ops.rs ~4199) AND the generic/F32/F16/literal-f64 path (~4233, via sort_key/compare_sort_keys). Fix: seed ±inf, pick if (cmp-strict OR v.is_nan()) for float dtypes; integer paths unaffected (no NaN). Narrow (only -NaN); +NaN already correct.
close_reason: argmax/argmin now match JAX _ArgMinMaxReducer (IEEE compare + sign-agnostic first-NaN) shipped 682fc482; fixes -NaN miss and ±0.0 tie-break; regression test vs jnp verified

title: Fix fj-ad JVP entries Erfc/ErfInv/Pow widening tangents to F64

Replace with scalar_constant_matching_dtype(...,&tangents[0]).
notes: Replaced Value::scalar_f64 with scalar_constant_matching_dtype(...,&tangents[0]) in Erfc, ErfInv, and Pow JVP entries. fj-ad 283 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean. The remaining Value::scalar_f64 calls in fj-ad/src/lib.rs are in test code (lines 12289+), trigamma_value, and other contexts where F64 is intentional.

title: fj-conformance lacks IRFFT VJP coverage for Complex64 input
description: The Complex64 silent-zero bug in scale_hermitian_adjoint (frankenjax-7ckd) survived previous reviews because no conformance test exercised the IRFFT VJP path for Complex64 (f32 complex) input — every existing IRFFT gradient case uses Complex128. Acceptance: (a) add a test in crates/fj-conformance/tests/ad_numerical_verification.rs (or a new file) that builds a single-equation Jaxpr with Primitive::Irfft on a Complex64 input tensor, evaluates fj_ad::grad_jaxpr / grad_jaxpr_with_cotangent against it, and verifies the result against a small JAX-equivalent reference (the existing AD numerical-verification harness style); (b) cover both an even fft_length (has a Nyquist bin to keep untouched) and an odd fft_length (no Nyquist); (c) compare gradient values within a relaxed-f32 tolerance (~1e-5) since the input dtype is f32-complex; (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added 2 IRFFT VJP tests for Complex64 input: even fft_length (verifies gradient nonzero AND matches finite-diff within f32 tolerance) + odd fft_length (no-Nyquist branch nonzero check). Documents that IRFFT VJP currently widens to Complex128 internally (dtype-widening is orthogonal follow-up; this bead's scope is silent-zero regression coverage). fj-conformance ad_numerical_verification 60 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 28bd935.

title: Replace AD unsupported-primitive placeholder wording
description: mock-code-finder audit found crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs renders AdError::UnsupportedPrimitive as 'AD not implemented for primitive: <name>'. This unsupported derivative path is a real fail-closed contract, but the phrase 'not implemented' looks like placeholder/stub code and is not currently covered by the no-stub regression display guard. Replace the message with explicit unsupported-primitive wording and extend crates/fj-conformance/tests/no_stub_regression.rs to ban generic 'not implemented' and cover AdError::UnsupportedPrimitive display so future placeholder wording cannot silently re-enter.
close_reason: Replaced AD unsupported primitive placeholder wording and extended no_stub_regression to ban generic not-implemented wording.

title: fj-core Jaxpr::validate_well_formed does not recurse into equation sub_jaxprs
description: crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:1271 Jaxpr::validate_well_formed iterates only self.equations top-level — it never recurses into equation.sub_jaxprs (the bodies attached to control-flow primitives like Cond, Scan, While). Malformed sub_jaxprs (duplicate bindings, unbound input vars, outvars not declared) are not caught at validation time and surface much later as interpreter/eval errors, or worse, silently corrupt control-flow execution. Acceptance: (a) extend validate_well_formed to call self.validate_well_formed() recursively on every equation.sub_jaxprs entry; (b) propagate any nested JaxprValidationError up to the caller (a nested error reuses the top-level error type — no new variants needed); (c) add a unit test that builds a parent Jaxpr whose Cond equation has a sub_jaxpr with DuplicateBinding on its invars, and asserts validate_well_formed returns the nested error; (d) also assert that well-formed parents containing well-formed sub_jaxprs still pass; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch. Note: validate_well_formed's existing return type already supports recursion (Result<(), JaxprValidationError>) so this is a non-breaking semantics tightening.
close_reason: Extended Jaxpr::validate_well_formed to recurse into equation.sub_jaxprs via sub_jaxpr.validate_well_formed()?. Sub-jaxpr scopes remain independent (no parent bindings propagated). 2 new tests: nested DuplicateBinding propagates + well-formed body passes. Full workspace tests green; fj-core 116 tests; clippy clean. Committed 4715e38.

title: Fix fj-lax eval_unary_elementwise scalar path to preserve input dtype
description: crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:1225 in eval_unary_elementwise scalar arm returns Value::scalar_f64(op(value)) regardless of input dtype — silently widens F32/BF16/F16 scalars to F64 for every unary transcendental (Exp, Log, Sin, Cos, Tan, Sinh, Cosh, Tanh, Asin, Acos, Atan, Asinh, Acosh, Atanh, Lgamma, Digamma, Erf, Erfc, ErfInv, etc.). Fix: encode the result at the input literal's dtype using a small match (F32→from_f32, BF16→from_bf16_f32, F16→from_f16_f32, F64→from_f64, integer/bool→keep as_f64 path since these primitives may have already promoted). Add regression test asserting Exp(F32 scalar 1.0) returns F32Bits. Workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: eval_unary_elementwise scalar arm now preserves input literal dtype (F32→from_f32, BF16/F16 via half-precision helpers, F64 unchanged; int/bool fall back to F64). Brings scalar branch in line with the dtype-preserving tensor branch. Unblocks F32 end-to-end dtype tests latent from elpz. fj-lax 761 + ad_numerical_verification 75 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 2bbe9c2.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: cyclic Jacobi for gram_pseudoinverse (pinv/lstsq) — 2.15x
description: gram_pseudoinverse (backing pinv/lstsq) still used the classic max-pivot jacobi_eigendecomposition (O(d^4): O(d^2) off-diagonal scan before every rotation) while SVD/eigh real paths already moved to jacobi_eigendecomposition_cyclic. Output G+ = Sum (1/lambda_i) v_i v_i^T is eigenpair-order-invariant so the swap is drop-in; both solvers converge to the same spectrum to machine precision, pinv/lstsq stay tolerance-equal. Bench linalg/pinv_192x192_f64 208.5ms->96.9ms (2.15x), pinv_256x128 61.4->35.6ms (1.72x). linalg_oracle 56/56, pinv/lstsq 7/7.

title: [infra] cargo test (debug) broken via rch: stale memchr in shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR (toolchain drift)
description: rch exec -- cargo test -p <crate> (debug profile) fails project-wide with: E0514 'found crate memchr compiled by an incompatible version of rustc 1.95.0-nightly (a33907a7a 2026-02-14)' at /data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/deps/libmemchr-acf1cea59ddad64a.rmeta, breaking serde_json (and thus most test builds). The shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR (/data/tmp/cargo-target) holds debug deps built by an older nightly than the current rust-toolchain.toml pin; cargo is reusing the stale .rmeta instead of rebuilding. clippy and release-profile builds/benches are unaffected (separate artifacts) — workaround for validation: cargo test --release -p <crate>. FIX: clean the stale debug artifacts in the shared target (e.g. cargo clean -p memchr, or a full debug clean) so they rebuild under the current toolchain; consider not sharing CARGO_TARGET_DIR across toolchain versions. rch status also shows reduced capacity (6/10 workers; vmi1167313 critical memory+storage pressure; ts1 unreachable).
close_reason: Resolved: shared-target stale debug memchr .rmeta rebuilt under current nightly. Verified: 'cargo test -p fj-core' debug = 127 passed; 'cargo test -p fj-conformance --no-run' (serde_json->memchr consumer) builds clean in debug. No repo code change — was artifact staleness in /data/tmp/cargo-target. Root cause (unpinned nightly + shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR across toolchain versions) is rch-infra, will recur until target isn't shared across nightlies; recovery is 'cargo test --release' workaround or rebuild.

title: Fix fj-core infer_dtype_from_repeated_literal mismapping Complex64/Complex128 to F64
description: At crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:1107-1109, the helper maps F64Bits | Complex64Bits | Complex128Bits → DType::F64. The F64 mapping is correct, but Complex literals are silently downgraded to DType::F64. Used by TensorValue::repeat_axis0 (line 941) — so repeat_axis0 on a Complex64 scalar produces a tensor declaring DType::F64 while containing Complex64Bits elements (invariant violation). Map Complex64Bits → DType::Complex64 and Complex128Bits → DType::Complex128.
notes: Fixed infer_dtype_from_repeated_literal at fj-core/src/lib.rs:1098 to return DType::Complex64/Complex128 for Complex64Bits/Complex128Bits literals instead of collapsing them to DType::F64. Used by TensorValue::repeat_axis0 — so repeating a complex scalar now produces a tensor with the correct declared dtype. Added regression test repeat_axis0_preserves_complex_scalar_dtype. fj-core 117 pass, clippy clean. (Note: pre-existing unrelated failure in committed_gate_logs_use_stable_script_replay_commands due to README artifact hash drift.)

title: eval_eig silently drops imaginary part of complex input matrix
description: eval_eig (linalg.rs ~2793) does a.iter().map(|(re,_im)| re) then eig_qr_iteration(&a_real,n), computing eigendecomposition of the REAL PART only for a complex input — silently wrong vs jnp.linalg.eig which accepts complex matrices. Unlike det/solve (fixed gi9hg/n8qom), this needs a COMPLEX QR / Hessenberg algorithm (the real eig_qr_iteration is real-only) — a substantial lift. P2: complex non-Hermitian eig is less common; the complex HERMITIAN path (eval_eigh) already works via complex Jacobi. Options: complex Hessenberg + shifted QR, or document/reject complex eig until implemented (fail-closed beats silent-wrong).
close_reason: Implemented cdd1ccee: eval_eig now uses complex_eig_qr for complex input (unshifted complex QR via modified Gram-Schmidt -> fully-triangular complex Schur form, eigenvalues on diagonal; inverse iteration for eigenvectors) instead of dropping the imaginary part. Verified A=H·T·H with T=diag(2+i,-1+3i,-2i): spectrum recovered + A·v=λ·v residual <1e-7. 1152 lib + 56 conformance pass. Completes the complex-linalg parity cluster (solve gi9hg, det/slogdet n8qom, eig gx9mm).

title: fj-ad lacks forward-mode JVP for non-symmetric eig (JAX has eig_jvp_rule)
close_reason: Implemented 93135f20: eig JVP in jvp_rule_multi — M=V⁻¹dA V (V⁻¹ via complex Solve), dλ=diag(M), dV=V(F∘M), F[i,j]=1/(λ_j-λ_i). Verified by self-consistency dA·V+A·dV=dV·diag(λ)+V·diag(dλ) for a complex-pair spectrum {1±i,3}. VJP stays fail-closed (JAX has no eig VJP). 72 jvp + 19 ad-oracle + 325 fj-ad pass.

title: non-symmetric eig (eval_eig) returns wrong eigenvalues/vectors for n>=3 (V1 unshifted-QR stub)
description:
eig_qr_iteration (crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs ~2615) is a 'V1 correctness' stub:
Hessenberg reduction + UNSHIFTED QR (fixed 100 iters) then extracts eigenvalues from
the DIAGONAL only (line ~2684) and returns the accumulated Schur vectors q_total as
'eigenvectors'. Two bugs for n>=3:
(1) COMPLEX eigenvalues wrong. Unshifted QR converges to real Schur form (quasi-
    triangular: 1x1 + 2x2 blocks); a complex-conjugate pair stays a 2x2 block whose
    subdiagonal never vanishes, so the max_subdiag<1e-10 check never trips (always 100
    iters) and reading the diagonal yields the block's real diagonal, not the pair.
    Repro: A = block-diag([[0,-1],[1,0]], 2) has eigenvalues {i,-i,2}; eval_eig returns
    ~{0,0,2}.
(2) EIGENVECTORS wrong. Schur vectors (columns of Q) are NOT eigenvectors unless T is
    diagonal; unshifted QR gives upper-quasi-triangular T, so q_total columns fail
    A v = lambda v for non-diagonal n>=3. Untested: all assert_eig_residual cases in
    linalg_oracle.rs are n=2 (handled by a separate direct 2x2 formula) or n=3 DIAGONAL.
Also slow: always 100 O(n^3) iters, no shift/deflation, poor accuracy on clustered
spectra (unshifted QR converges only linearly).

FIX (alien-artifact, the LAPACK dgeev path):
 - Francis implicit DOUBLE-SHIFT QR on the Hessenberg form (Golub&VanLoan Alg 7.5.2):
   trailing-2x2 double shift, Householder bulge-chase, deflation when subdiagonals
   vanish -> real Schur form fast (quadratic) and complex-pair-safe without complex
   arithmetic.
 - Eigenvalue extraction: walk T, 1x1 -> real, 2x2 -> complex pair via the 2x2 formula.
 - Eigenvectors: inverse iteration on (A - lambda I) (needs a complex Gaussian solver;
   none exists today — eval_solve rejects complex), 1-2 iters per eigenvalue; or LAPACK
   dtrevc-style back-substitution on T then transform by Q.
 - Verify against constructed A = Q T Q^T with KNOWN 1x1/2x2 blocks (ground truth, no
   numpy) for real AND complex spectra; residual A v = lambda v < 1e-9.
Behavior-parity impact: eval_eig diverges from jnp.linalg.eig for any n>=3 with complex
eigenvalues or non-trivial eigenvectors.
notes:
PARTIAL FIX 9f136448 — EIGENVALUES now correct for n>=3 (incl. complex pairs).
eig_qr_iteration now iterates unshifted Hessenberg QR until quasi-triangular (no two
consecutive non-negligible subdiagonals, LAPACK relative criterion) and extracts
eigenvalues by walking 1x1 (real) and isolated 2x2 (real-or-complex via trace/disc)
blocks — exact per block. Ground truth: eig_qr_recovers_complex_eigenvalues_n3
(A=H·T·H similar to block-diag(2,[[0,-3],[3,0]]) -> {2,±3i}). 1147 fj-lax lib + 56
linalg conformance pass.

STILL OPEN (eigenvectors): eval_eig returns the Schur vectors (q_total), which are
NOT eigenvectors unless T is diagonal, so eigenVECTORS are wrong for any n>=3
non-diagonal (real or complex). Also unshifted QR is only linearly convergent —
accurate for well-separated moduli/small n but slow/weak for clustered spectra.
NEXT: (a) Francis implicit double-shift for robustness+speed; (b) eigenvectors via
inverse iteration on (A-λI) (needs a complex Gaussian solver — none today) or
dtrevc-style back-substitution on the real Schur form then transform by Q. Keep this
bead open until eigenvectors + a residual test for n>=3 complex land.
close_reason: Fixed: eigenvalues (9f136448, real-Schur 2x2 extraction) + eigenvectors (50b9d59d, inverse iteration on A-λI via complex_linear_solve). Residual A·v=λ·v verified for n=3 {2,±3i} and n=4 mixed {5,-1,1±2i}; 14 eig tests pass.

Fixed circular oracle: i0e(1)/i1e(1) asserted the old A&S values at 1e-10 tol; now assert true scipy/JAX values at 1e-12 + new 8-point accuracy table. Verified bit-exact vs scipy.special.i0e/i1e.

title: Fix fj-lax eval_unary_elementwise tensor F32 element widening (dtype/element mismatch)
description: Tensor arm at crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:1272-1276 routes BF16->from_bf16_f32 and F16->from_f16_f32 but the catch-all '_ => Literal::from_f64(mapped)' captures F32 as well. So F32 tensors declare DType::F32 (per line 1262) but their element literals are F64Bits — same invariant violation as 2chb but for F32 in eval_unary_elementwise. Add an explicit DType::F32 arm using from_f32.
notes: Added explicit DType::F32 arm in eval_unary_elementwise tensor element constructor so F32 tensors now contain F32Bits literals matching their declared dtype (was previously producing F64Bits elements in F32-declared tensors). fj-lax 761 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal erases subnormal overflow
description:
Review evidence:
- Commits 2971d17, 348837a, 2ab703f, and 1ccef8f moved several unsafe egraph identities behind OptimizationConfig::aggressive().
- safe_algebraic_rules still contains reciprocal-reciprocal: (reciprocal (reciprocal ?a)) => ?a.
- That identity is not safe for tiny nonzero floats: 1.0 / 1e-320 overflows to +Inf, then 1.0 / +Inf is +0.0, while the rewrite returns 1e-320.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test building chained_unary_jaxpr(Primitive::Reciprocal, Primitive::Reciprocal).
2. Evaluate original and optimize_jaxpr_with_config(..., &OptimizationConfig::safe()) with Value::scalar_f64(1e-320).
3. Assert optimized output matches original bit/value semantics.
4. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_egraph_recip RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph reciprocal_reciprocal -- --nocapture
5. Current safe optimizer can extract ?a and return 1e-320 instead of the original +0.0.
close_reason: Moved reciprocal-reciprocal to numerically_unsafe_rules; subnormal overflow breaks identity

Fix: thread scalar_constant_matching_dtype through all three. Add erf_vjp_numerical_f32 test asserting F32 dtype is preserved.
close_reason: Threaded scalar_constant_matching_dtype through Erf/Erfc/ErfInv VJPs (2/√π, -2/√π, √π/2 constants). fj-lax scalar eval_unary_elementwise still widens non-complex scalars to F64 internally (latent issue beyond scope) so end-to-end F32 dtype preservation needs that follow-up. erf_vjp_numerical_f64_value_sanity pins f64 value correctness. 75 ad_numerical_verification tests pass; clippy clean. Committed ae76379.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: BLIS-style panel packing for matmul_2d (large-n bandwidth bound)
description:
matmul_2d already has a 4x4 register-blocked microkernel (matmul_2d_row_block, ~29 GFLOP/s single-core at 256^3 = near autovectorized scalar peak). 256^3 is compute-bound (B=512KB L2-resident). But 512^3 is MEMORY-BANDWIDTH bound: threaded only 2.68x over single-core (77 vs 29 GFLOP/s) despite min(nproc,64) threads. Root cause: each row-block streams all of B (2MB > L2); aggregate B traffic ~ (m/MR)*k*n*8 = ~256MB from L3/RAM, saturating bandwidth so extra threads don't scale.

LEVER (BLIS 5-loop): pack B into KC x NC panels and A into MC x KC panels in contiguous scratch buffers so the microkernel streams contiguous, L2/L1-resident memory. Cuts B re-reads from RAM. Classic high-perf GEMM; target 1.5-2x at 512^3+ (and cascades to Cholesky/LU/QR trailing updates, conv2d im2col GEMM, pinv Gram solves — all call matmul_2d).

BIT-IDENTICAL: packing only COPIES elements; the microkernel still accumulates each C[i][j] over k in ascending order, so matmul_2d_ikj_bit_identical_to_ijk / _large / _threaded all still hold. Prove via those existing tests + a 512^3 before/after.

Blocking params to tune (cache-fit): KC ~ 256 (A panel MCxKC + B panel KCxNC fit L2), NC ~ n, MC ~ 256. Start single-thread packed kernel, confirm >=2x at 1024^3, then re-add row-block threading on top (each thread packs its own A panel, shares packed B). Watch: scratch alloc per call (reuse a thread-local/passed buffer). NOTE 256^3 is already compute-bound so won't improve — measure at 1024^3 where B (8MB) deeply spills L3.
notes:
ATTACKED column-parallel + B-panel-packing (thread owns a column range, walks all m rows, packs k×ncb B-strip contiguous). REJECTED — regresses at ALL sizes: 256³ 1.12→1.23ms, 512³ 3.60→5.33ms, 1024³ 13.05→16.23ms (code stashed: "frankenjax-cc: REJECTED matmul column-parallel+pack").

ROOT CAUSE: bandwidth premise was WRONG. B (8MB) is L3-RESIDENT on this 64-core box, so the row-block kernel's CONTIGUOUS row-major B streaming is already L3-efficient. Column-parallel forces each thread to read a STRIDED vertical B-strip; even packed contiguous, the strided pack-read + per-thread alloc + lost contiguous stream nets a regression. A "resident" panel strided in memory is not enough.

CORRECTED: the ~5.5x/64-core limit at 1024³ is L3/inter-CCD-fabric bandwidth for shared B, NOT RAM bandwidth, NOT the microkernel. Scaling improves with size (256³~1x, 512³ 2.5x, 1024³ 5.5x) so small GEMM is also spawn/overhead-bound (matmul_2d spawns fresh OS threads PER CALL).

NEXT (all different from column-pack): (1) shared pre-packed B once before fan-out; (2) NUMA/CCD-aware B replication (likely real unlock on EPYC, non-portable); (3) persistent worker pool to kill per-call spawn (helps many-small-GEMM: blocked Cholesky/LU/conv trailing updates). Per no-ceiling 'go different', deprioritize vs other primitives — GEMM single-kernel + register-tile already done.
close_reason: Rejected duplicate GEMM packing lane: existing evidence already rejected packed B panels (pass83), direct/full column panels (pass84), full RHS transpose (pass87), and MR/NR register replay variants (pass120); inherited MR8 candidate was manually restored after same-worker ts1 benchmark showed 512 midpoint 4.271->3.719 ms but 1024 midpoint 17.751->20.029 ms, below Score>=2.0. Next primitive is not another bit-exact packing replay: file/attack a behavior-contract bead for FP-reordering blocked/recursive/communication-avoiding GEMM, or pivot to the next profile-backed non-GEMM hotspot.

title: fj-ad: scatter VJP returned 2 grads for 3 inputs — updates input got NO gradient
description: Scatter inputs are [operand, indices, updates] but the VJP returned only [grad_operand, grad_updates] (2). In backward, zipping 3 inputs with 2 cotangents put grad_updates onto the (non-diff) indices slot and left the updates input with ZERO gradient. So reverse-mode AD through scatter / .at[idx].set(updates) gave no gradient to the update values. Fixed: return [grad_operand, zeros_like(indices), grad_updates] (one per input). Gather/dynamic_slice were fine (their non-diff inputs are LAST so the short return zips correctly); scatter's non-diff indices is in the MIDDLE.
close_reason: Fixed: scatter VJP returns [grad_operand, zeros_like(indices), grad_updates] (3, one per input). Updates input now receives gather(g, indices) instead of nothing. Test vjp_scatter_zeros_overwritten_positions updated to assert arity 3 + grads[2]=updates grad. fj-ad 345/0, conformance ad 93/0.

title: Fix fuzzer input-count placeholders for control flow and multi-input primitives
description:
WHAT: Fix the hardcoded arity=1 placeholder for 10 primitives in crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257-268. The comment "Default placeholder" confirms these are wrong.
WHERE: The function primitive_input_count() at approximately line 200 of common.rs.
HOW: Set correct input counts based on each primitive definition:
  - DynamicUpdateSlice: 3 (operand, update, start_indices)
  - OneHot: 1 (indices tensor; num_classes is a param)
  - Cumsum: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Cumprod: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Sort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Argsort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Conv: 2 (lhs, rhs; strides/padding are params)
  - Cond: 3+ (predicate, true_operands, false_operands)
  - Scan: 2+ (init_carry, xs)
  - While: 1+ (init_state)
Note: Cond/Scan/While also need sub-jaxpr generation in the fuzzer, which is a harder problem. For this task, at minimum fix the numeric arity. Sub-jaxpr fuzzing can be a follow-up.
WHY: With arity=1, the fuzzer generates malformed inputs for these primitives, meaning they always fail at arity validation and never exercise the actual primitive logic. This is wasted fuzzing cycles.
TESTING: Run the fuzzer briefly to confirm it no longer panics on arity mismatches for these primitives.
ACCEPTANCE: The "Default placeholder" comment is removed. Each primitive has a documented, correct input count.
close_reason: Implemented real primitive arity counts for parameterized conformance fuzz targets; added regression test; verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --all-targets

title: [perf] fj-dispatch: lazy concat output for contiguous batched gather

One lever: for contiguous batched-operand gather in clip/promise modes, emit a TensorValue backed by LiteralBuffer::from_concat_slices over the operand buffer slices instead of copying literals. Preserve batch order, prepared-index order, resolved-index clipping, shape/dtype, and all fallback behavior; keep fill/drop and partial-slice paths on the existing copy implementation. Required proof: current-output vs lazy-output bit equality for shared and batched indices, golden sha256, same-worker RCH rebench, crate-scoped fj-dispatch test/check/clippy/fmt/UBS; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: dense-I64/lazy-output batched gather candidate preserved focused tests but regressed same-worker RCH ts1 vmap_gather/batched_operand_shared_indices 17.397 us -> 541.61 us and batched_operand_batched_indices 18.043 us -> 540.05 us; source diff reverted. title: fj-lax QR: custom no-pack register-blocked GEMM microkernel (matmul_2d unsuitable for QR)
description: ALL matmul_2d-based QR blocking is now definitively REJECTED (bead 7nosl closed): panel b=32 (461ms), b=128 (349ms), AND recursive Elmroth-Gustavson (recursive halving, large-b GEMMs at top, direct per-block compact-WY T) = 327ms — all SLOWER than the 84ms scalar per-reflector baseline. Recursive E-G was implemented + VERIFIED CORRECT (qr_blocked_reconstructs_and_orthonormal_large n=300: A=QR + QtQ=I to 1e-9; small-n bit-identity preserved) but 4x slower. ROOT CAUSE: matmul_2d's per-call packing + thread-spawn overhead dominates the MANY small-b GEMMs the recursion produces at deep levels (b shrinks to LEAF), and even the top large-b GEMMs run at ~6 GF/s (worse than scalar 18 GF/s) due to spawn/pack per call. The scalar rank-1 trailing update (apply_real_householder_columns, already 8-col-tiled) is memory-bound but has NO per-call overhead, so it wins at this size. The ONLY remaining QR perf path: a CUSTOM lightweight GEMM for qr_block_apply's two products (Vt*C and V*W2) that is single-threaded + NON-packing + register-blocked (f64x4 SIMD microkernel, MR x NR register tile), avoiding matmul_2d's spawn/pack entirely, so the BLAS-3 memory-pass reduction (b reflectors per pass vs b passes) actually materializes. Reuse the verified recursive driver (qr_factor_recursive/qr_form_q_recursive/qr_compact_wy_t in orphan f37b96b4 + this session's revert) — only swap matmul_2d for the custom kernel. Major multi-session microkernel effort; the recursive scaffolding is done + correct.

title: Implement complex pow in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex exponentiation to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: The match arm at line 160 currently returns "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" for all binary ops except add/sub/mul/div.
HOW: Complex pow uses the identity (a+bi)^(c+di) = exp((c+di)*ln(a+bi)). Decompose: r=|z|, theta=atan2(b,a), ln(z)=(ln(r), theta), then exp of the product. For integer exponents, use repeated squaring for efficiency.
WHY: JAX supports complex pow natively. Without it, grad(f) fails for any traced program that involves complex exponentiation.
TESTING: Add tests in fj-lax for complex pow at several points: (1+i)^2 = 2i, (1+i)^(0.5), purely real complex pow matches real pow. Add numerical AD verification for complex pow VJP.
ACCEPTANCE: The "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" error no longer triggers for Primitive::Pow with complex inputs.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex pow in fj-lax with integer fast path and direct evaluator tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: Conv panics on zero stride parameter
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs accepts strides=0 for Primitive::Conv and then performs division by that stride instead of returning a typed EvalError. In 1D conv, eval_conv_1d parses params["strides"] at lines 2817-2820 and then uses width.div_ceil(stride) for SAME padding at line 2824 or (width - kernel_w) / stride for VALID padding at line 2835. In 2D conv, parse_stride_pair returns zero at lines 2976-2986 and compute_output_and_pad divides by stride at lines 2997 and 3005.

Repro: construct lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 3, 1] }, three F32 literals) and rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 1, 1] }, one F32 literal), set params { "strides": "0", "padding": "valid" }, then call eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Current debug/test builds panic on division by zero; release builds can also compute invalid output geometry.

Expected: reject zero or malformed strides before arithmetic with a typed EvalError::Unsupported such as "conv stride must be positive". Add regression tests for 1D VALID, 1D SAME, and 2D zero-stride inputs.
close_reason: Rejected zero and malformed Conv strides before output geometry arithmetic

Preserve exact key/counter mapping [0,i], output order, f64 scaling expression, no FMA/reassociation, and golden RNG digests. Required: RCH baseline, RCH rebench same-worker if possible, threaded-vs-current bit identity, random determinism conformance, golden sha256, check/clippy/fmt/UBS.

title: Fuse partially-overlapping Rev∘Rev pairs in egraph shape-chain prepass
description: optimize_shape_parametric_chains currently cancels Rev∘Rev only when both equations specify the exact same axis set (after sort/dedup via rev_axes). Two consecutive Rev equations on a single-use intermediate with axis sets A and B that overlap partially are equivalent to one Rev with axis set (A ⊕ B) — symmetric difference — because each axis in A ∩ B is reversed twice (no-op) while each axis in A ⊕ B is reversed exactly once. Acceptance: (a) extend fuses_shape_chain with (Rev, Rev) gated on a non-empty symmetric difference and add a sym-diff path in the fuse branch that rewrites current's axes param to the deduplicated sorted symmetric difference; (b) the existing cancellation branch already handles A == B, so the new fuse path only fires for partial overlap; (c) add unit tests for partial overlap (rank-3 [0,1] then [1,2] -> [0,2]) and for the empty-overlap case ([0] then [1] -> [0,1]); (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Implemented Rev∘Rev partial-overlap fusion: new rev_axes_symmetric_difference returns Some(sym-diff) iff both sides parse; extended fuses_shape_chain with (Rev, Rev) gated on non-empty sym-diff (cancellation branch handles A==B case); fuse branch rewrites current's axes param to sym-diff CSV. Added 2 unit tests (rank-3 partial overlap [0,1]+[1,2]->[0,2] and disjoint [0]+[1]->[0,1]) with eval-equivalence assertions. Verified: fj-egraph 134 tests, conformance preserves_semantics 33 tests, clippy clean. Committed 036a25a.

title: fj-cache FileCache::path_for trusts CacheKey::as_string for filename — add path-traversal guard
description: crates/fj-cache/src/backend.rs:132 FileCache::path_for builds a cache file path via self.cache_dir.join(format!('{}.bin', key.as_string())). Because CacheKey is a public struct with pub fields and as_string returns 'namespace-digest_hex', a maliciously-constructed key with digest_hex containing path separators or '..' could traverse outside cache_dir on write or read. The canonical build_cache_key always produces hex digests, but defense-in-depth at the boundary prevents future regressions if a caller hand-rolls a key. Acceptance: (a) add a free helper fn 'fn is_safe_cache_filename(s: &str) -> bool' that returns true iff every char is ASCII alphanumeric, '-', '_', or '.'; (b) in FileCache::get/put/evict, reject keys whose as_string() fails this check by either returning None / not persisting / counting a put_failure or evict_failure as appropriate; (c) add a unit test that constructs a CacheKey with digest_hex='../foo' and asserts: put silently rolls a put_failure, get returns None, evict returns false without touching disk; (d) verify the normal hex-digest path still passes via existing round-trip tests; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Added is_safe_cache_filename allowlist (ASCII alphanumeric + '-_.'). FileCache::get returns None, put bumps put_failures, evict returns false for unsafe keys. 3 new tests cover positive cases, separator/control-byte/non-ASCII rejection, and end-to-end put/get/evict refusal on a '../etc/passwd'-style key. fj-cache 90 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 763ba10.
