title: Harden BufferView slice range overflow
description: Multi-pass bug hunt found crates/fj-runtime/src/buffer.rs BufferView::slice computes offset + len before validating bounds. For adversarial or saturated usize inputs this can panic in debug builds due integer overflow, and in optimized builds can wrap before the subsequent slice expression panics or misclassifies the range. Replace the addition with checked_add so out-of-range or overflowing slices return None as documented. Add a regression test using usize::MAX to prove BufferView::slice is panic-free and returns None for overflowing ranges. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-runtime, rch cargo check -p fj-runtime --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-runtime --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Hardened BufferView::slice with checked_add so overflowing offset+len returns None without panic; regression test covers usize::MAX overflow; workspace fmt/check/clippy/test pass on current HEAD.

title: integer_pow primitive missing Complex64/Complex128 support
description:
## Summary
IntegerPow primitive rejects complex dtypes with "expected numeric tensor elements".

x^n for integer n is well-defined for complex numbers via repeated multiplication:
- (a+bi)^2 = a^2 - b^2 + 2abi
- i^2 = -1, i^3 = -i, i^4 = 1

This is a basic operation needed for complex polynomial evaluation.

## Evidence
- commit b666c5f: added 4 ignored tests documenting the gap

## Fix Required
Implement complex integer_pow via complex multiplication.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 98f46aa - added complex integer_pow support

title: ReduceWindow same padding is offset incorrectly
description: eval_reduce_window computes SAME output length with ceil(input/stride) but starts every window at out_index * stride, so padding is effectively only on the high side. JAX/XLA SAME semantics split total padding with low padding floor(total/2), so examples like input [1,2,3,4,5], window=3, stride=1 should produce [3,6,9,12,9] instead of [6,9,12,9,5]. Implement low-padding offsets for SAME in rank-N reduce_window and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Implemented centered SAME padding offsets for reduce_window and added rank-1/rank-2 regressions

title: [modes-of-reasoning] numerical-stability-vs-JIT-correctness
description:
E-graph rewrites (exp-log, div-self, reassociation) are mathematically valid but numerically treacherous near domain boundaries. Eager vs JIT diverge on edge cases (0, denormal, NaN, Inf). Conformance detects drift post-hoc but cannot prevent unsafe rewrites.

Key locations:
- fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:269-324 (rewrite rules without domain guards)
- fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:219-222 (underflow-prone complex reciprocal)
- fj-conformance/src/numerical_stability.rs:857-920 (tolerance enforcement gap)

Suggested work:
1. Add domain predicates to e-graph rules (div-self only when provably non-zero)
2. Implement numerical safety mode that disables associativity rewrites
3. Extend conformance gate to compare JIT vs eager on edge-case inputs
close_reason: Added OptimizationConfig with numerical_safety_mode flag in ceea42f. Safe mode disables 6 unsafe rewrites: exp-log, div-self, mul-reciprocal, div-mul-cancel, log-product, log-quotient.

title: Pin Reciprocal infinity signed-zero bit oracle coverage

Add to_bits coverage for reciprocal(+inf) and reciprocal(-inf) without touching fj-lax.
close_reason: Pinned Reciprocal infinity signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with RCH: cargo test -p fj-conformance --test reciprocal_oracle.

title: Implement lax.associative_scan
description: JAX's associative_scan performs parallel prefix scans using associativity. Current scan is sequential. Needed for efficient parallel reductions.
close_reason: Implemented associative_scan with sequential V1 algorithm. 6 tests pass. Control flow at 100%.

title: Pin Floor signed-zero oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX lax.floor uses numpy.floor in lax_reference and lowers to stablehlo.floor, preserving IEEE signed zero: floor(+0.0) returns +0.0 and floor(-0.0) returns -0.0. FrankenJAX conformance currently checks floor(+/-0.0) only by numeric equality. Add exact signed-zero bit-pattern oracle coverage in fj-conformance.
close_reason: Pinned floor signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test floor_oracle.

title: UBS: refactor test-log failure wrappers away from panic!
description: Follow-up from frankenjax-gn6. UBS samples panic! in shared test-log wrappers in crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs and crates/fj-interpreters/src/partial_eval.rs where caught failure_detail is rethrown with panic! after artifact logging. Replace with a helper/macro pattern that records failure evidence while preserving test failure semantics, or explicitly isolate this as test-only infrastructure so UBS no longer reports it as production critical.

title: Implement lax.linalg.eig (non-symmetric)

title: [perf] Pass 7 profile fj-cache cache-key subsystem
description: Run /extreme-software-optimization on fj-cache after fresh rch hyperfine/Criterion baseline. Target only a profiler-backed cache-key/cache-lookup hotspot. Keep at most one lever scoring >=2.0 with isomorphism proof and golden sha256; otherwise close with no-code rejection evidence.

title: [perf] fj-ad: avoid single-output backward cotangent Vec allocation
description: Profile-backed target from fj-ad Criterion baseline on 2026-06-02 via RCH worker vmi1227854, command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-ad-profile-baseline cargo bench -p fj-ad --bench ad_baseline -- --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1. Ranked rows: ad/grad_poly_x3+x2+x mean 1.8119 us [1.6817,1.9843] (top), ad/value_and_grad_poly mean 1.8041 us [1.7302,1.8964], ad/grad_sin_cos_mul mean 1.3047 us [1.2479,1.3773], ad/jvp_poly_x3+x2+x mean 1.0723 us [1.0429,1.1023]. Code-level hotspot hypothesis: backward() allocates a Vec<Value> for output cotangents on every tape entry, even for single-output primitives (Add/Mul in the top polynomial reverse-mode path). Opportunity score: Impact 3 x Confidence 4 / Effort 1 = 12. Acceptance: one lever only; preserve multi-output VJP handling; record golden sha256/isomorphism proof; rebench on RCH and keep only if score >= 2.0.
close_reason: Completed. Implemented single-output backward cotangent fast path in fj-ad (code present in f2bcc448), preserving multi-output VJP path. Profile baseline via rch Criterion: initial top row ad/grad_poly_x3+x2+x mean 1.8119 us on vmi1227854. Same-worker paired proof on vmi1149989: grad_poly 1.5482 us -> 1.4565 us (-5.92%); value_and_grad_poly 1.4870 us -> 1.3807 us (-7.15%); grad_square 532.67 ns -> 506.58 ns (-4.90%). Golden sha256 3d9d98165d6bde73ac90d5e066a414db1ca0475249fa1be5d998986b90e2a0a4 verified. Validation: rch cargo check/test/clippy -p fj-ad passed; ubs reports existing fj-ad monolith inventory only.

title: Refactor fj-conformance FFT/IFFT Complex64 dtype tests to use validate_dtype_consistency
description: Two more matches! loops in ad_numerical_verification.rs (lines 1700-1705 FFT, 1745-1750 IFFT). Each iterates VJP cotangent elements and matches!(elem, Complex64Bits(..)). Replace with .validate_dtype_consistency() on the VJP tensor for consistency with the recent test-refactor wave (go38/n43l/u46o/etnz/1vw5).
notes: Replaced two matches!(elem, Complex64Bits(..)) iteration loops (FFT and IFFT Complex64 VJP tests) with .validate_dtype_consistency() calls. Also removed the now-unused use fj_core::Literal::Complex64Bits imports in both tests. ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: Pin Sqrt signed-zero oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX lax.sqrt uses numpy.sqrt in lax_reference and lowers to stablehlo.sqrt, preserving IEEE signed zero: sqrt(+0.0) returns +0.0 and sqrt(-0.0) returns -0.0. FrankenJAX conformance currently checks sqrt(+0.0) only by numeric equality and lacks a sqrt(-0.0) bit-pattern oracle. Add exact signed-zero coverage in fj-conformance.
close_reason: Pinned sqrt signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test sqrt_oracle.

title: [perf] fj-dispatch: per-signature compilation cache (memoize executable plan) — kill ~4.2us fixed per-call pipeline overhead
description: Profile (pass90, artifacts/performance/evidence/fj_dispatch_profile_pass90_2026-06-04.json): every jit/vmap/grad dispatch() pays ~4.2us fixed overhead even for a trivial scalar_add — it rebuilds BackendRegistry+Box<CpuBackend>, runs verify_transform_composition + simulate_nested_trace_contexts + build_cache_key_ref, and unconditionally constructs the evidence ledger + effect_trace/nested_trace_frame_trace strings, on EVERY call. cache_key is already computed (build_cache_key_ref) but nothing is memoized. LEVER (JAX jit model, bit-exact): memoize the traced+transformed executable plan keyed by the existing cache_key so repeat dispatch of the same signature skips trace/transform/composition-proof, while STILL emitting the per-call audit ledger (e2e_p2c004_evidence_ledger_audit_trail must stay byte-identical). Target >=2x on dispatch_latency/jit/scalar_add (4.2us -> <2us). Discipline: isomorphism + golden-sha256 on outputs AND evidence ledger. Deeper follow-on: compiled-interpreter lowering (flat opcode/closure stream + register-allocated variable slots) to remove eval_jaxpr's per-equation FxHashMap + primitive re-dispatch (~117ns/eqn). Companion algorithm-swap track (separate, parity-revalidation not isomorphism): closed-form 3x3 eigh / register-blocked small QR/SVD for vmap_eigh/svd/qr (241/169/136us).
close_reason: Pivoted + partially delivered. The dispatch() per-call cost is dominated by REQUIRED OUTPUT generation (the DispatchResponse evidence_ledger's format! strings + the SHA256 cache_key), not redundant recomputation a cache could skip — execute_with_transforms for a plain jit just calls backend.execute, and the audit ledger (e2e_p2c004_evidence_ledger_audit_trail) must be rebuilt every call — so a per-signature compilation cache cannot cleanly reach >=2x there. Pivoted to the interpreter execution-model layer instead: pass91 (commit pending) replaces the fj-interpreters FxHashMap<VarId,Value> environment with a flat Vec<Option<Value>> slot array (different memory layout) + allocation-free scratch resolve + direct single-output eval_primitive dispatch => 1.74x on eval_jaxpr_throughput/chain_add/1000 (67.3us vs 117us), bit-exact (dense_and_hashed_envs_agree + 131 tests). The >=2x follow-on (compiled interpreter) is filed as a new bead.

title: [perf] fj-dispatch lazy scatter updates pass63
description: Profile-backed pass60 target after pass59 re-profile via RCH Criterion on vmi1293453 (2026-06-03): vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates 328.28 us [315.71,344.53], next rows vmap_scan/functional_sub_jaxpr_add_emit_128x64 283.54 us and vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 282.41 us. Previous pass37 rejected constant-operand initialization; do not repeat that lever. Alien primitive: nested data-parallel segmented arrays plus vectorized row-major update stream. Target one structural lever in crates/fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs: avoid per-batch eval_primitive scatter slicing/stacking for the profiled overwrite/update shape if ordering and duplicate-index last-write semantics can be preserved exactly. Baseline/rebench via rch crate-scoped; prove ordering/tie-breaking/FP/RNG and golden sha256; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Completed: lazy repeated-patch scatter output lowered RCH Criterion vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates from 328.28 us [315.71,344.53] to 23.507 us [23.412,23.616] mean (13.97x); score 12.5; behavior proof covers ordering, duplicate-index last-write, F64Bits NaN payload/-0.0 preservation, no RNG; golden artifact artifacts/performance/evidence/fj_dispatch_lazy_scatter_pass63_2026-06-03.json sha256 71718e90b90c6f95379a180c70711cf8fe3c2ed178bf6c398df0cd7b2d129001.

description: Rev allocated vec![0;rank] per output element + Vec<Literal>; reusable-odometer gather into dense storage is bit-exact and removes the per-element allocation.
close_reason: Shipped pass106. eval_rev dense path: shared rev_gather<T> with a reused coordinate odometer (no per-element vec![0;rank] alloc, incremental advance) gathering reversed elements into dense storage (new_*_values) for F64/I64/Bool; generic path also uses rev_gather. Bit-exact (rev_dense_matches_generic f64/i64 multi-axis + 13 rev tests pass; clippy clean). eval/rev_256x256_f64 1587.1us->331.80us = 5.0x worker-corrected (calibrated by ReduceSum). Score 10.0.

title: Add Bessel I0e/I1e conformance tests
close_reason: done

title: [perf] fj-dispatch: reuse Eigh Jacobi row scratch
description: Pass75 profile-backed target: after Pass74, focused RCH rebench on ts2 still leaves vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 at 371.56 us [368.92,373.73], above the unchanged SVD/QR rows from the full dispatch profile. Existing jacobi_eigendecomposition_matrix allocates new_row_p and new_row_q Vecs inside every Jacobi iteration for every batched 3x3 matrix. One safe-Rust lever only: allocate those row scratch buffers once per decomposition and reuse them each iteration, preserving matrix scan order, Jacobi pivot selection, row update order, eigenvalue total_cmp ordering, floating-point operations, RNG absence, and fallback/error surfaces. Baseline/rebench via rch crate-scoped; golden sha256 + isomorphism proof required; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Completed Pass75: fj-dispatch Jacobi Eigh row scratch reuse; vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 same-worker RCH baseline 371.56us [368.92,373.73] -> 308.67us [307.93,309.51] on ts2; speedup 1.204x mean, 1.192x conservative; score 15.0; golden sha256 bcb7a043a581d165097ff63b7dc9caba550b1ed4a724a1f3d208254e31523e21; fj-dispatch test/check/clippy/fmt/UBS clean

title: Expose scalar Array __float__ conversion in fj-py
description: Upstream JAX Array implements __float__ for scalar arrays via core.check_scalar_conversion and rejects non-scalars. fj-py PyValue/Array currently lacks float(array) support. Add __float__ for scalar numeric values with vector rejection and Rust/Python smoke coverage.
close_reason: Completed fj-py scalar Array.__float__ conversion with vector rejection and Rust/Python smoke coverage

title: Align reduce_window padding modes
description: JAX reduce_window accepts VALID, SAME, and SAME_LOWER padding strings and rejects unknown padding. fj-lax/fj-trace currently treat unknown padding as valid and miss uppercase/SAME_LOWER behavior. Add fail-closed parsing plus tests.
close_reason: Completed reduce_window VALID/SAME/SAME_LOWER padding parsing with fail-closed unknown padding, trace validation, and conformance coverage.

title: Add fj-lax unary-elementwise F32/BF16 tensor dtype-invariant regression tests
description: The recent fixes 2chb (eval_unary_int_or_float tensor BF16/F16) and eldm (eval_unary_elementwise tensor F32) corrected silent dtype/element-kind mismatches. Add small inline unit tests in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs that assert: (1) Exp on an F32 tensor produces F32Bits element literals, (2) Sqrt on an F32 tensor produces F32Bits element literals, so future regressions are caught at the test level rather than only surfacing as downstream gradient widening.
notes: Added two regression tests in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs tests module: unary_exp_f32_tensor_preserves_dtype and unary_sin_f32_tensor_preserves_dtype. Each asserts (1) the output tensor's declared dtype is DType::F32 and (2) every element literal is F32Bits — pinning the dtype/element invariant restored by eldm. fj-lax now 763 (was 761), clippy clean.

title: Remove redundant literal_matches_dtype wrapper in fj-conformance dtype_promotion test
description: After 26kv landed Literal::matches_dtype as a fj-core public method, the test-local literal_matches_dtype wrapper at crates/fj-conformance/tests/dtype_promotion_oracle_parity.rs:118 is just a trampoline. Remove it and inline .matches_dtype(...) at the one callsite.
notes: Removed the trampoline literal_matches_dtype wrapper in dtype_promotion_oracle_parity.rs and inlined .matches_dtype(...) at the single callsite. fj-conformance::dtype_promotion_oracle_parity 6 pass, clippy clean.

title: Vectorize BatchTrace QR batching
description: Replace generic per-slice BatchTrace fallback for QR with a direct batched matrix decomposition rule and parity/performance tests.
close_reason: Completed direct QR BatchTrace batching rule with parity tests and workspace validation

title: fj-ad: slogdet VJP scales A^-T by sign cotangent (gs[0]) not logabsdet cotangent (gs[1]) -> silent-zero grad; JVP missing for 2-output slogdet
close_reason: slogdet VJP now scales A^-T by the logabsdet cotangent gs[1] (was gs[0]=sign cotangent -> silent-zero grad). slogdet JVP moved to jvp_rule_multi returning both tangents [d_sign=0, d_logabsdet=tr(A^-1 dA)]; single-output jvp_rule arm removed (2-output slogdet routes to jvp_rule_multi). Tests: VJP vs FD of logabsdet with a poison sign-cotangent proving gs[0] is ignored; JVP both-tangents FD-verified.

title: Add XLogY negative-domain conformance oracle coverage
description: Extend fj-conformance XLogY oracle tests to cover upstream JAX scipy.special.xlogy semantics over vector lanes: x == 0 masks invalid log(y) lanes to zero, x != 0 with y < 0 yields NaN, and positive/negative x with y == 0 produce signed infinities.
close_reason: Completed XLogY negative-domain conformance oracle coverage

title: erf primitive missing Complex64/Complex128 support
description:
## Summary
Erf (error function) primitive rejects complex dtypes with error:
"erf is not supported for complex dtypes"

JAX supports complex erf via the Faddeeva function w(z):
- erf(z) = 1 - exp(-z^2) * w(i*z)
Important for quantum mechanics and signal processing.

## Evidence
- commit 557e148: added 4 ignored tests documenting the gap
- Tests: erf_oracle.rs oracle_erf_complex64_*, oracle_erf_complex128_*

## Fix Required
Implement complex error function using Faddeeva function or series expansion.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ed7da23 - added complex erf support

title: Add Sinc conformance tests
description: Add oracle conformance tests for Sinc primitive. sinc(x) = sin(x)/x with sinc(0)=1. Test F64 dtype with edge cases.
close_reason: done

title: Expose CPU-local device helpers in fj-py
description: Parity gap in claimed fj-py domain: upstream JAX exports device_put, device_get, and block_until_ready. fj-py has CPU-local PyValue objects but lacks these public helpers. Add fj.device_put(value), fj.device_get(value), and fj.block_until_ready(value) that preserve PyValue data for current CPU-local execution, with scalar/vector smoke coverage.
close_reason: Implemented fj-py device_put, device_get, and block_until_ready for CPU-local PyValue execution with Rust/Python scalar and vector smoke coverage. cargo fmt --check -p fj-py passed; UBS on fj-py files exited 0. rch cargo check/test -p fj-py --all-targets attempted but blocked before fj-py by shared fj-lax Igamma/Igammac type errors and fj-trace primitive exhaustiveness.

title: Speed integer_pow tensor eval
description: Optimize fj-lax eval_integer_pow tensor evaluation by replacing collect-based Result<Vec<_>> construction with a preallocated push loop, preserving F64 output dtype, exponent parsing, and numeric error semantics. Benchmark with IntegerPow on a 1k f64 tensor.
close_reason: Completed: eval_integer_pow tensor path now preallocates output and pushes mapped literals directly instead of collecting Result<Vec<_>>; eval/integer_pow_1k_f64 improved from 21.778 us to 7.1557 us median while preserving exponent parsing, F64 output dtype, and numeric error semantics; verified with integer_pow oracle/unit tests, workspace check, clippy, tests, fmt, diff-check, and UBS.

title: Implement rank-2 dot eval parity
description: fj-trace infers dot for vector/matrix combinations, but fj-lax eval_dot still rejects rank-2 tensors. Implement rank (2,1), (1,2), and (2,2) dot evaluation with row-major semantics and focused tests.
close_reason: Implemented rank-2 dot eval parity with matrix-vector, vector-matrix, and matrix-matrix coverage

title: fj-ad Pad VJP zeros complex gradients and widens real inputs to F64
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2053-2150 Pad VJP extracts operand gradient elements via 'g_tensor.elements[flat].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0)' and rebuilds as DType::F64. Same silent-zero + widening pattern as Cumsum/scan/dynamic_update_slice VJPs. Complex inputs become all-zero F64; real inputs (F32/BF16/F16) widen to F64. Pad VJP also returns 'Value::scalar_f64(g_total_sum - op_grad_sum)' for pad_value gradient — wrong dtype for complex inputs. Acceptance: (a) preserve dtype for operand gradient by copying Literal values verbatim when the position is in-range; use zero_literal_for_dtype for out-of-range positions; (b) build operand gradient with input's dtype, not hardcoded F64; (c) compute pad_value gradient with dtype-aware accumulator (complex: (re,im) pair, real: f64 then re-encoded to input dtype); (d) regression test for Complex64 input verifying operand gradient stays Complex64 and not all-zero; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Pad VJP now preserves gradient dtype: operand gradient copies Literal values verbatim with zero_literal_for_dtype for out-of-range positions, output dtype = g_tensor.dtype. Pad-value gradient tracks operand-feeding g positions via BTreeSet, then sums remaining (padding) positions in dtype-appropriate domain ((re,im) pair for Complex; f64 for real with re-encode to input dtype). Scalar fallback uses zeros_like(g) for pad_value. New regression test pins Complex64 operand grad correctness + Complex64 pad_value sum = (16, 17). fj-ad 278 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 127616e.

title: [perf] fj-dispatch scatter constant operand initialization hotspot
description: Profile-backed pass 26 follow-up. Fresh rch Criterion profile on 2026-06-02 via fj-dispatch dispatch_baseline on worker vmi1227854: vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates mean 14.385 ms (CI 12.968..16.308 ms), next hotspot after gather. Candidate lever: rank-1 scatter overwrite fast path currently initializes each batched output row by repeatedly extending the same unbatched operand; for constant operands this can be initialized with a single repeated literal while preserving scatter overwrite order, duplicate-index semantics, floating-point/RNG behavior, and output shape. Must establish focused rch baseline before edit and keep only if Score>=2.0.
notes: REJECTED lever (negative result, 2026-06-02, agent cc). Profiled via rch Criterion on remote workers. Baseline (current code, extend_from_slice replication of operand rows): vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates = 12.49 ms [12.34..12.66]. Proposed lever (constant-operand fast path: vec![first; output_len]): 24-26 ms across 3 runs [23.57..26.69] = ~2x REGRESSION. Root cause: Literal is #[derive(Copy)] (fj-core/src/lib.rs:636), so extend_from_slice lowers to copy_from_slice/memcpy (bulk), whereas vec![lit; N] has no from_elem specialization for an enum and falls back to an element-wise clone loop. Score < 0. Also: the 12.5 ms is dominated by dispatch/evidence-ledger machinery + per-iter input cloning, NOT operand init (524288xi64 memcpy ~0.4ms), so operand-init was never the real hotspot. Any future revisit must profile the dispatch path itself, not the rank-1 overwrite fill.
close_reason: Rejected: constant operand initialization candidate failed keep threshold; focused baseline vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates 21.542 ms on vmi1153651, after 22.902 ms on same worker.

title: [perf] fj-dispatch segmented batched-operand gather pass59
description: Profile-backed pass58 target from RCH Criterion on vmi1293453 (2026-06-03): vmap_gather/batched_operand_batched_indices 609.56 us, batched_operand_shared_indices 548.59 us, vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates 350.83 us, vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 292.12 us. Current batch_gather only fast-paths unbatched operand + batched indices; batched operand cases fall through to batch_passthrough_leading, slicing/evaluating/stacking 128 separate gathers. Alien primitive: Nested Data Parallelism / Flattening Transform (§4.6) plus Vectorized Execution (§8.2): treat operand [B,N,...] as segmented array and copy gathered slices directly to [B,index_shape...,slice...] in one row-major pass. Constraints: one lever in crates/fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs, preserve JAX gather index_mode semantics, ordering, tie-breaking N/A, floating point bit identity by copying Literal values, RNG N/A, prove via existing gather tests + golden sha256, rebench via rch Criterion and keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Completed pass59 segmented batched-operand gather direct path. RCH Criterion vmap_gather shared 548.59 us -> 23.297 us, batched 609.56 us -> 22.070 us; score 20.0; golden sha256 f9c21a6dc2a8453292e556dde06b17e057f94951011af990eb333dad74df9dad; fj-dispatch check/test/clippy passed.

Added 4 rules to numerically_unsafe_rules: sinh(asinh(x))=>x, asinh(sinh(x))=>x, tanh(atanh(x))=>x, atanh(tanh(x))=>x. Added 2 tests verifying the rules simplify composed expressions.

title: Expose Array __len__ behavior in fj-py

Add __dlpack_device__ returning the CPU DLPack tuple for concrete fj-py Array values and cover it in Rust/Python smoke tests.

title: [perf] fj-dispatch rank2 vmap in_axes=1 direct row-major path
description: Post-pass125 RCH ts2 reprofile at commit 7d70a42f found the top disjoint fj-dispatch hotspot: dispatch_latency/vmap/rank2_add_one_in_axes_1 mean 42.084us [41.333,42.786], after vmap_scan was reduced to 12-20us. Candidate primitive: replace remaining nonzero-axis rank2 vmap loop/stack overhead with a direct row-major strided elementwise execution path for effect-free AddOne-style scalar body, preserving mapped-axis order, row-major output order, vmap_in_axes/vmap_out_axes semantics, shape/errors, f64 operation order, tie/RNG N/A. Baseline first via RCH, one lever only, golden sha256, keep only Score>=2.0.

Same-worker RCH ts2 dispatch_latency/vmap/rank2_add_one_in_axes_1 42.795us [42.236,43.410] -> 21.841us [21.657,22.062], 1.959x midpoint / 1.914x conservative; Score 16. Isomorphism: output[col,row]=source[row,col].wrapping_add(scalar), dtype/shape/fallback/error behavior unchanged, FP/RNG/ties N/A. Golden sha256 29d48dbffb8ba3bc95e29416bbe470cd18432ffd471b8ec7ef068c3b7a61b3ef; evidence artifacts/performance/evidence/fj_dispatch_axis1_i64_dense_output_pass126_2026-06-05.*. Validation: focused test_in_axes_1, cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets, clippy -p fj-dispatch --all-targets --no-deps -D warnings, rustfmt, golden sha256, jq, git diff --check, UBS exit 0. Full fj-dispatch lib gate is blocked by unrelated Eigh eigenvector sign assertions at batching.rs:7325.

comments.text: Reopened: Replacing stale rejection closeout after pass126 found and kept a measured dense-output lever.

title: Add Cummax/Cummin conformance oracle coverage
description: Parity gap discovered by diffing implemented FrankenJAX primitives against upstream JAX lax cumulative reductions: upstream exposes lax.cummax and lax.cummin, and fj-lax implements Primitive::Cummax/Cummin, but fj-conformance cumulative_oracle.rs only covers cumsum/cumprod. Add focused oracle coverage for 1-D, axis, reverse, duplicate extrema, and dtype-preserving behavior.
close_reason: Completed Cummax/Cummin cumulative conformance coverage. Verified with RCH remote ts1: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test cumulative_oracle (31 passed). UBS: timeout 120 ubs crates/fj-conformance/tests/cumulative_oracle.rs exit 0; rustfmt --check on touched file exit 0.

title: vmap(cumsum/cumprod/cummax/cummin) rejected negative axis (eval normalizes it)
description: batch_cumulative parsed the cumulative axis with parse_param_usize (rejects negative end-relative axes) and shifted +1 unconditionally, but eval_cumulative normalizes negatives. So vmap(cumsum, axis=-1) errored 'invalid usize'. Same class as batch_reduce negative-axis (dis3l). Fixed: parse axis as i64; non-negative shifts +1, negative left unchanged (end-relative, batch prepended at front) and eval normalizes vs the batched rank.
close_reason: Fixed: batch_cumulative parses axis as i64; negative axis preserved. Test test_batch_cumsum_handles_negative_axis passes. batching suite 183/0.

title: Expose Array shape as tuple in fj-py
description: Upstream JAX Array.shape returns a tuple[int, ...] via basearray/ArrayImpl metadata, while fj-py PyValue/Array currently returns a Python list. Return tuple shape from the Python property and update smoke coverage for scalar and vector Array values while keeping internal Vec storage.
close_reason: Completed Array shape tuple parity in fj-py

title: Pin Floor fractional vector positive-zero bit oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX lax.floor delegates to stablehlo.floor and the legacy lax_reference maps floor to numpy.floor; numpy.floor(0.1/0.5/0.9) returns +0.0 with bit pattern 0x0. FrankenJAX conformance already pins scalar floor(+/-0.0) signed-zero bits, but tensor fractional lanes still check only numeric equality for 0.0. Add vector-lane bit-exact +0.0 assertions in fj-conformance without touching fj-lax.
close_reason: Pinned vector floor zero bit assertions for positive zero/fractional lanes and verified with RCH: cargo test -p fj-conformance --test floor_oracle.

title: fj-cache LruCache/TtlLruCache hide FileCache failure counters from external monitors
description: frankenjax-xda6/wwki added put/evict/clear failure counters to FileCache. Code that wraps FileCache in LruCache or TtlLruCache (which IS the typical production path) cannot reach those counters because the wrapper exposes the trait surface only and stores inner: B as a private field. Acceptance: (a) add three default-implemented methods to CacheBackend trait: 'fn put_failure_count(&self) -> u64 { 0 }', 'fn evict_failure_count(&self) -> u64 { 0 }', 'fn clear_failure_count(&self) -> u64 { 0 }'. Default impl returns 0 so existing implementors (InMemoryCache, external impls) require no change; (b) override the three methods on FileCache to forward to the AtomicU64 counters (the existing inherent methods stay for backwards compat); (c) override the three methods on LruCache and TtlLruCache to delegate to self.inner.put_failure_count() etc.; (d) add a unit test that wraps a FileCache in LruCache, triggers a put failure via a missing parent directory, and asserts the LruCache surface reports put_failure_count() == 1; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added default-implemented put_failure_count/evict_failure_count/clear_failure_count to CacheBackend trait (default = 0). Overrode on FileCache to forward to AtomicU64 counters, and on LruCache/TtlLruCache to delegate to self.inner. 2 new tests: LruCache forwards FileCache put_failures + InMemoryCache returns 0 defaults. fj-cache 92 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed a1c098f.

title: Conv SAME padding panics on empty spatial dimensions
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs Conv SAME padding assumes the computed output spatial dimension is nonzero. For empty spatial axes, width.div_ceil(stride) or input_size.div_ceil(stride) can return 0, then line 3123 and helper line 3324 compute out_w - 1 / out - 1 and underflow in debug/test builds. Repro 1D: lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F64, Shape { dims: vec![1,0,1] }, vec![]), rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F64, Shape { dims: vec![1,1,1] }, vec![1.0]), params { padding="same", strides="1" }, then eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Expected: either an empty output tensor with shape [1,0,1] or a typed EvalError, but no panic. Actual: SAME geometry computes out_w=0 and underflows at ((out_w - 1) * stride + kernel_w). The 2D path uses compute_output_and_pad and has the same out == 0 underflow for any empty height/width with SAME padding. Add regression tests for 1D width=0 and 2D height/width=0.
close_reason: Fixed: added early return for input_size=0 in compute_output_and_pad and 1D conv path. Added 5 regression tests for empty spatial dims.

title: Hoist reduce_window input strides
description: eval_reduce_window recomputes row-major input stride multipliers inside every window element. Precompute checked input strides once per call and reuse them in flat-index calculation while preserving overflow errors and iteration order.
close_reason: Hoisted reduce_window input stride calculation, added reduce_window Criterion baseline, and aligned the transform fixture with typed I64 reduce_window output

title: Replace e-graph not-yet-supported comment with principled UnsupportedPrimitive categorization
description:
CONTEXT: crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:598 has comment Shape ops require params -- not yet supported followed by 34 primitives returning UnsupportedPrimitive. The not yet wording is misleading -- these are excluded BY DESIGN.

CHANGES REQUIRED:
  1. Replace comment with accurate doc: these primitives are architecturally excluded because the s-expression egraph cannot represent parameterized operations (shapes, axes, sub-jaxprs).
  2. Split the 34-primitive catch-all into categorized groups with per-group doc comments:
     - Shape manipulation (13): Reshape, Slice, DynamicSlice, Gather, Scatter, Transpose, BroadcastInDim, Concatenate, Pad, Rev, Squeeze, Split, ExpandDims
     - Linear algebra (5): Cholesky, Qr, Svd, TriangularSolve, Eigh
     - FFT (4): Fft, Ifft, Rfft, Irfft
     - Control flow (4): Cond, Scan, While, Switch
     - Sorting (2): Sort, Argsort
     - Convolution (1): Conv
     - Index/utility (3): Iota, BroadcastedIota, DynamicUpdateSlice
     - Type conversion (2): BitcastConvertType, ReducePrecision
     - Cumulative (2): Cumsum, Cumprod
     - Windowed (1): ReduceWindow
     - Encoding (1): OneHot
  3. Add ExclusionReason enum or string field to EGraphLoweringError::UnsupportedPrimitive so error messages say e.g. reshape excluded: requires shape parameters (not representable in algebraic e-graph).
  4. Add test: for each category, verify the error message contains the category reason.

TESTING: Existing egraph tests must still pass. New test: construct Jaxpr with each excluded primitive, call jaxpr_to_egraph, assert error message contains category keyword. Log all 34 exclusion messages.

ACCEPTANCE: The string not yet supported gone. Each exclusion has a documented reason. Error messages are informative.
close_reason: Centralized egraph exclusion metadata by category, surfaced reasons in UnsupportedPrimitive errors, added full exclusion-message coverage, and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-egraph plus workspace check/clippy/fmt

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax GEMM2048: packed-A slab + JC-blocked macro-kernel
description: Follow-up after rejected frankenjax-o84i1 paired-panel grouping. Profile-backed target remains linalg/matmul_2d_2048x2048x2048_f64; latest fixed-worker baseline on vmi1293453 was [368.53, 377.68, 388.09] ms and paired-panel candidate measured [335.84, 357.62, 386.10] ms but failed conservative/Score gate. Alien primitive: true packed-A slabs plus JC-blocked macro-kernel / cache-oblivious dense GEMM. One safe-Rust lever in crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_contraction.rs: reduce A reloads and C reload/store pressure more substantially than panel grouping, while preserving per-output ascending-k accumulation, output row/column order, thread row ownership, dtype/shape/error behavior, FP bits, tie behavior, and RNG surface. Required: focused RCH baseline before edits, golden sha256, isomorphism proof, same-worker RCH rebench with Score>=2.0 or restore source and record rejection.
close_reason: Shipped packed-A GEMM2048 slab: vmi1156319 baseline 2.1227..2.2458..2.3819s -> 1.4692..1.6003..1.7549s, median 1.403x, conservative 1.210x, Score 8.0; RCH focused matmul proof/check/clippy passed; golden sha256 623dc08e5918e3fa5de7a0abf45b2db7cd9cd8cb02a6c98314e4a73c9b21e39c.

title: Fix fj-core infer_dtype_from_literals mismapping all-Complex literals to F64
description: Companion to e8g4. infer_dtype_from_literals at crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:1054 checks for all-I64/U32/U64/Bool/BF16/F16/F32 — but the final else falls through to DType::F64 for both F64 AND Complex64/Complex128 literal lists. Used by TensorValue::stack_axis0 (line 900). Add explicit all-Complex64 and all-Complex128 branches; keep F64 as the else for the genuine F64/mixed case.
notes: infer_dtype_from_literals now returns DType::Complex64 for all-Complex64Bits lists and DType::Complex128 for all-Complex128Bits lists rather than falling through to DType::F64. Added stack_axis0_preserves_complex_scalar_dtype regression test. fj-core now 118 tests (was 117). clippy clean.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax Cholesky1024: wider CA panel for packed Schur updates
description: Profile-backed after post-pass152 reprofile on RCH ts1: linalg/cholesky_1024x1024_f64 = 52.267..54.642..57.350 ms, highest median in the current fj-lax linalg group; linalg/lu_1024x1024_f64 = 51.689..53.789..55.855 ms, qr_1024x1024_f64 = 34.825..35.438..36.677 ms, matmul_2d_1024x1024x1024_f64 = 23.180..27.994..39.649 ms. Alien-graveyard primitive: communication-avoiding dense linear algebra / cache-aware packed panels. One lever: change CHOLESKY_BLOCK_SIZE from 128 to 256 so n=1024 uses fewer panels and the first trailing Schur update crosses the existing packed-B GEMM path instead of the generic no-pack 128-wide update. Proof obligations: small n < threshold unchanged; SPD/NaN/error behavior unchanged; large blocked path remains tolerance-validated by scalar parity + reconstruction; ordering/tie/RNG surfaces documented; golden sha256; keep only same-worker Score >= 2.0, otherwise restore source and record rejection.
close_reason: Rejected: Cholesky 256-panel CA lever regressed same-worker ts1 cholesky_1024 baseline 54.524..55.442..56.465 ms to 68.519..69.272..70.052 ms (0.800x median, 0.778x conservative), Score 0.0 < 2.0. Focused Cholesky proof passed; source restored; evidence in fj_lax_cholesky_panel256_zoxtq_pass153_2026-06-06.rejected.json.

title: Repair fj-conformance fuzz coverage for pmap enum additions
description:
testing-fuzzing audit finding from cod_4.

Evidence:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_cod_4_fuzz RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml` fails with E0004 in `crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257`: missing `Primitive::Psum`, `Primitive::Pmean`, `Primitive::AllGather`, `Primitive::AllToAll`, and `Primitive::AxisIndex`.
- `fj-core::Primitive` defines those pmap collective variants at `crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:196-201`; `common.rs` `ALL_PRIMITIVES` and `primitive_arity` only cover pre-pmap variants.
- `fj-core::Transform` includes `Transform::Pmap`, but `sample_transform` in `common.rs:241-246` samples only `Jit`, `Grad`, and `Vmap`, so dispatch/composition fuzzing never exercises the V1 pmap fail-closed path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update the fuzz helper manually so primitive sampling and arity handling are exhaustive for the current `Primitive` enum, including pmap collectives.
- Include `Transform::Pmap` in transform fuzz sampling with expectations that pmap dispatch fails closed rather than panicking.
- Add/extend helper-level tests or compile checks so future enum additions break clearly.
- Verify with `rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml`.
close_reason: Fixed: fuzz helper now covers pmap collective primitives and samples Transform::Pmap; added fuzz_common test target. Verified cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml --all-targets and cargo test --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml --test fuzz_common via rch. cargo test -p fj-conformance is blocked by unrelated stale README artifact hash in e2e_forensic_log_contract.

title: Add NaN/Inf gradient propagation tests for AD edge cases
description:
CONTEXT: The AD engine (fj-ad) has no tests verifying how NaN and Inf propagate through VJP/JVP rules. JAX propagates NaN faithfully: grad(sin)(NaN) = NaN, grad(exp)(Inf) = Inf. Silent NaN-to-zero or Inf-to-zero conversion in gradient rules would be a correctness bug that goes undetected.

SCOPE: Add targeted tests in crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs verifying:
  1. NaN propagation: grad(sin)(NaN)=NaN, grad(cos)(NaN)=NaN, grad(exp)(NaN)=NaN, grad(log)(NaN)=NaN, grad(tanh)(NaN)=NaN, grad(x^2)(NaN)=NaN
  2. Inf propagation: grad(exp)(Inf)=Inf, grad(log)(0+)=Inf or -Inf, grad(x^2)(Inf)=Inf, grad(1/x)(0)=(-Inf or NaN)
  3. Mixed: grad(x*y) where x=NaN, y=finite
  4. Backward pass with NaN cotangent: vjp(sin, 1.0, NaN) should produce NaN gradient
  5. JVP with NaN tangent: jvp(sin, 1.0, NaN) should produce NaN output tangent

TESTING: Each test checks is_nan() or is_infinite() on the gradient output. Use descriptive assertion messages. Log each test case.

ACCEPTANCE: All NaN/Inf propagation tests pass. No gradient rule silently converts NaN to 0 or Inf to finite.
close_reason: Already covered by AD NaN/Inf propagation tests; verified in full workspace test run

title: Implement Hypot primitive
description: Add Hypot primitive (sqrt(x^2 + y^2)) to fj-core, fj-lax eval, and fj-ad gradients. JAX: lax.hypot. Computes hypotenuse with numerical stability for large/small values.
close_reason: done

title: Extend scalar dtype promotion oracle to half precision
description: dtype_promotion_matches_jax still excludes f16 and bf16 scalar cases as a known gap. Include f16/bf16 in the scalar oracle set, update the stale gap comment, and fix any implementation mismatch revealed by the JAX fixture.
close_reason: Extended scalar dtype promotion oracle coverage to f16/bf16; dtype_promotion_matches_jax now exercises 128 cases and workspace gates pass.

title: Pin Cbrt signed-zero bit oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX lax.cbrt / NumPy cbrt preserves IEEE signed zero: cbrt(+0.0) = +0.0 and cbrt(-0.0) = -0.0. cbrt_oracle.rs currently checks zero with tolerance or equality plus sign rather than exact bit patterns. Add exact bit-pattern coverage in scalar and tensor cases without touching fj-lax.
close_reason: Pinned Cbrt signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with RCH: cargo test -p fj-conformance --test cbrt_oracle.

title: Pin Neg signed-zero bit oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX jnp.negative is a NumPy ufunc wrapper that lowers to lax.neg, which is lowered to HLO negate; NumPy/JAX negate flips IEEE signed-zero bits, so neg(+0.0) must produce -0.0 and neg(-0.0) must produce +0.0. fj-conformance currently only accepts numeric zero for Primitive::Neg and has no exact signed-zero bit coverage. Add bit-pattern tests in neg_oracle.rs without touching fj-lax.
close_reason: Pinned Neg signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with RCH: cargo test -p fj-conformance --test neg_oracle.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax Cholesky1024 lower-triangle Schur update
description: Profile-backed target after packed-A GEMM keep: RCH ts1 linalg/cholesky_1024x1024_f64 [119.25, 121.50, 126.40] ms, top row above pinv_256x128 [110.58, 113.26, 119.61] ms and matmul_2d_2048 [104.69, 106.73, 110.28] ms. Alien primitive: symmetric rank-k / lower-triangle-only Schur update for blocked Cholesky. One safe-Rust lever in crates/fj-lax/src/linalg.rs: replace full A22 -= L21 L21^T GEMM with an update that computes only q<=p lower-triangle entries used by subsequent panels, preserving per-entry ascending c accumulation, panel order, diagonal/panel solve order, dtype/shape/error behavior, FP NaN surface, and RNG absence. Required: same-worker RCH focused baseline before edit, focused Cholesky proof + golden sha256, same-worker rebench, keep only Score>=2.0 or restore source and record rejection.
close_reason: Shipped lower-triangle-only Cholesky Schur update: ts1 baseline 106.28..117.61..130.30ms -> 89.706..92.497..95.659ms, median 1.271x, conservative 1.110x, Score 8.0; RCH focused Cholesky tests/check/clippy passed; golden sha256 2d4e2185e82faee371572306cb38a9bbba51dd972facb7401b0b61cac96e62ac.

title: Add Complex64 Rsqrt VJP/JVP test + thread dtype-aware constants
description: Like Sqrt (frankenjax-dgvr), Rsqrt VJP at fj-ad lib.rs:1287-1300 and Rsqrt JVP at 5892-5898 use scalar_value(-1.5)/scalar_value(-0.5) which are F64. Multiplied with Complex64 intermediates this widens to Complex128. Fix: replace with scalar_constant_matching_dtype(c, g/tangent) in both VJP and JVP. Add rsqrt_vjp_numerical_complex64 test pinning Complex64 dtype preservation.
close_reason: Threaded scalar_constant_matching_dtype through Rsqrt VJP (-1.5 + -0.5 constants vs g), Rsqrt JVP (same constants vs tangents[0]), AND Sqrt JVP ('2' constant vs tangents[0]) — finishes the Sqrt/Rsqrt VJP/JVP pair. rsqrt_vjp_numerical_complex64 test pins z=3+4i → g_z=-0.008+0.044i. fj-ad 283 + ad_numerical_verification 72 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 500a211.

title: Add Array delete state parity in fj-py
description: Upstream JAX Array exposes delete() and is_deleted() lifecycle methods. fj-py Array/PyValue currently lacks this API. Add CPU-local deletion state so new arrays report false, delete() marks them deleted, and is_deleted() reports true, with Rust and Python smoke coverage.
close_reason: Completed local Array delete() and is_deleted() state parity in fj-py

title: Reduce comparison vector allocation overhead
description: Optimize fj-lax tensor comparison evaluator hot paths by preallocating bool output vectors and pushing results directly instead of collecting iterator Result<Vec<_>>, preserving scalar/tensor comparison semantics and shape checks.
close_reason: Completed comparison preallocated output loop optimization; eq_1k benchmark improved about 50.4%

title: [perf][no-gaps] fj-lax: parallelize dense cumulative scan (cumsum/cumprod/cummax/cummin) over independent lines — 3x
description: eval_cumulative_dense scanned outer lines serially; the lines are independent so contiguous last-axis scans fan out across threads (scan_contiguous_lines_to_vec: forward push no-clone path, threaded whole-line block split, in-place for small/single). 3.04x on cumsum_4096x1024 many-lines; single-line still 1.34x (chunked parallel-prefix/Blelloch is the follow-up). Bit-exact: dense==literal + threaded==serial.
close_reason: Landed parallel cumulative scan from shared working tree: thread independent contiguous last-axis lines. cumsum_4096x1024 34.5->11.4ms = 3.04x; single-line 1.34x (parallel-prefix follow-up). 1100 fj-lax tests pass incl dense==literal + threaded==serial isomorphism.

title: fix: squeeze rejects a negative dimension (3-layer parity gap)
description: lax.squeeze canonicalizes negative dims via canonicalize_axis(i, ndim) (lax.py:3542), so squeeze(x, -1) is valid in JAX. frankenjax parsed 'dimensions' as usize at all 3 layers (fj-lax eval_squeeze, fj-trace shape inference, fj-dispatch batch_squeeze), rejecting negatives. Same class as expand_dims edq7y. Fixed: parse i64, normalize neg against input rank. Conformance oracle only tested non-negative dims (coverage gap that hid it).
close_reason: Fixed in this commit: i64 parse + negative-axis normalization across eval_squeeze (fj-lax), squeeze shape inference (fj-trace), batch_squeeze (fj-dispatch); oracle + batch negative-axis tests added.

title: Pin Sinh signed-zero oracle coverage
description: Upstream JAX lax.sinh lowers to chlo.sinh and its lax reference maps sinh to numpy.sinh, which preserves IEEE signed zero: sinh(+0.0) returns +0.0 and sinh(-0.0) returns -0.0. FrankenJAX conformance currently checks sinh(+/-0.0) only by numeric equality to zero. Add exact signed-zero bit-pattern oracle coverage in fj-conformance.
close_reason: Pinned sinh signed-zero bit oracle coverage and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test sinh_oracle.
